elements.text: 4606.4: Energy reports (11/06/24)
elements.label: section_header

elements.text: This section contains requirements related to:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Eligible energy reports
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Energy report conditions
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Energy report exceptions when proceeds are used to finance renewable energy sources
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Energy report alternatives
elements.label: text

elements.text: For Mortgages used to finance eligible improvements with an aggregate cost less than or equal to
elements.label: text

elements.text: $6,500, an energy report is not required. For Mortgages used to finance eligible improvements
elements.label: text

elements.text: with an aggregate cost greater than $6,500, an energy report must be obtained and retained in the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Mortgage file.
elements.label: text

elements.text: An energy report is not required for a “no cash-out” refinance Mortgage used to pay an Existing
elements.label: text

elements.text: Debt.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (a) Eligible energy reports
elements.label: text

elements.text: Energy reports must be one of the following:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Home Energy Rating Systems (HERS) report completed by a certified Residential
elements.label: text

elements.text: Energy Services Network (RESNET®) Home Energy Rater reflecting a HERS Index of
elements.label: text

elements.text: 90 or below (http://www.resnet.us/directory/search)
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Department of Energy Home (DOE) Energy Score Report completed by an independent
elements.label: text

elements.text: Home Energy Score Certified Assessor™ reflecting a DOE Home Energy Score of six or
elements.label: text

elements.text: greater (https://betterbuildingssolutioncenter.energy.gov/home-energy-score/home￾energy-score-map)
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Comparable rating report or audit completed by a certified home energy rater or
elements.label: text

elements.text: consultant indicating the property is a high-performing energy efficient property
elements.label: text

elements.text: (b) Energy report conditions
elements.label: text

elements.text: Energy reports must:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Identify the energy improvements and expected cost to complete these improvements
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Specify the actual or expected monthly or annual energy savings
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Verify that the energy improvements are cost effective. Energy improvements are cost
elements.label: text

elements.text: effective when the expected cost to complete these improvements, including
elements.label: text

elements.text: 4607.2: Eligible and ineligible CHOICERenovation®
elements.label: section_header

elements.text: Mortgages (Future effective date 07/01/26)
elements.label: text

elements.text: This section contains requirements related to:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Eligible Mortgages
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Ineligible Mortgages
elements.label: text

elements.text: (a) Eligible Mortgages
elements.label: text

elements.text: CHOICERenovation® Mortgages must be First Lien Mortgages and may be any Mortgage
elements.label: text

elements.text: eligible under the Seller’s Purchase Documents, unless specifically described as ineligible
elements.label: text

elements.text: below.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (b) Ineligible Mortgages
elements.label: text

elements.text: Mortgages with the following characteristics are not eligible for sale to Freddie Mac as
elements.label: text

elements.text: CHOICERenovation Mortgages:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Community Land Trust Mortgages
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Financed Permanent Buydown Mortgages
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Freddie Mac Enhanced Relief Refinance® Mortgages
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Government Mortgages
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Refi Possible® Mortgages
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Seller-Owned Converted Mortgages
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Seller-Owned Modified Mortgages
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Special purpose cash-out refinance Mortgages
elements.label: text

elements.text: 4607.3: Property eligibility and occupancy for
elements.label: section_header

elements.text: CHOICERenovation® Mortgages (06/04/25)
elements.label: text

elements.text: This section contains requirements related to:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Property eligibility
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Special occupancy requirement for CHOICERenovation® In Progress Mortgages
elements.label: text

elements.text: (a) Property eligibility
elements.label: text

elements.text: CHOICERenovation Mortgages must be secured by one of the following:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ 1- to 4-unit Primary Residence
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Second home
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ 1-unit Investment Property
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ A Manufactured Home, including a CHOICEHome® as described in Section 5703.12
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ A Condominium Unit, a unit located in a Planned Unit Development (PUD), or a
elements.label: text

elements.text: Cooperative Unit if permitted under the Seller’s Purchase Documents
elements.label: text

elements.text: (b) Special occupancy requirement for CHOICERenovation In Progress Mortgages
elements.label: text

elements.text: For CHOICERenovation In Progress Mortgages or CHOICEReno eXPress secured by
elements.label: text

elements.text: Primary Residences, if the Borrower does not occupy the Mortgaged Premises during the
elements.label: text

elements.text: course of renovations, the Mortgaged Premises will be deemed owner-occupied as of the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Delivery Date so long as the Borrower occupies the Mortgaged Premises as a Primary
elements.label: text

elements.text: Residence within 60 days of the last disbursement made and distributed from the Custodial
elements.label: text

elements.text: Account for Renovation Funds (as described in Section 4607.12).
elements.label: text

elements.text: 4607.4: General eligibility requirements for
elements.label: section_header

elements.text: CHOICERenovation® Mortgages (02/05/25)
elements.label: text

elements.text: This section contains requirements related to:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Completion Date and Settlement Date requirements
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Mortgage purpose
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Maximum loan-to-value (LTV)/total LTV (TLTV)/Home Equity Line of Credit (HELOC)
elements.label: text

elements.text: TLTV (HTLTV) ratios
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Determining value
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Secondary financing
elements.label: text

elements.text: (a) Completion Date and Settlement Date requirements
elements.label: text

elements.text: (i) Completion Date requirements
elements.label: text

elements.text: Renovations to the Mortgaged Premises must be completed by the Completion Date as
elements.label: text

elements.text: follows:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ 450 days after the Note Date for CHOICERenovation® Mortgages that are not
elements.label: text

elements.text: CHOICEReno eXPress® Mortgages
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ 180 days after the Note Date for CHOICEReno eXPress Mortgages
elements.label: text

elements.text: (ii) Completion Date extension
elements.label: text

elements.text: The Seller may request from Freddie Mac a Completion Date extension when
elements.label: text

elements.text: circumstances outside the Borrower’s or contractor’s control prevent completion of the
elements.label: text

elements.text: renovations by the Completion Date. The total days requested for the extension(s) must
elements.label: text

elements.text: not exceed 90 days.
elements.label: text

elements.text: To be eligible for a Completion Date extension, the Borrower must not:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Be delinquent at the time of the Seller’s extension request, and
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Have been 30 days delinquent more than once during the renovation period
elements.label: text

elements.text: To request a Completion Date extension, the Seller must submit to Loan Status Hub®
elements.label: text

elements.text: documentation supporting the reason for the delay (e.g., weather, supply chain issues,
elements.label: text

elements.text: permit delays, etc.). If Freddie Mac grants the extension, the Completion Date may be
elements.label: text

elements.text: extended:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ A maximum of 90 days for CHOICERenovation Mortgages
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ A maximum of 30 days for CHOICEReno eXPress Mortgages
elements.label: text

elements.text: If the extension request is granted, Freddie Mac will provide the extended Completion
elements.label: text

elements.text: Date through Loan Status Hub.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (iii)Settlement Date requirements
elements.label: text

elements.text: For CHOICERenovation Mortgages with Settlement Dates after completion of
elements.label: text

elements.text: renovations (see Section 4607.1(a)), the Settlement Date must occur no more than 450
elements.label: text

elements.text: days after the Note Date.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (b) Mortgage purpose
elements.label: text

elements.text: CHOICERenovation Mortgages must be either purchase transaction or “no cash-out”
elements.label: text

elements.text: refinance Mortgages with proceeds used as follows:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Purchase transaction Mortgages: Proceeds may be used to purchase the Mortgaged
elements.label: text

elements.text: Premises and to pay for the eligible renovations described in Section 4607.6
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ “No cash-out” refinance Mortgages: Proceeds may be used as described in Section
elements.label: text

elements.text: 4301.4, except that proceeds may not be used to disburse cash out to the Borrower. A
elements.label: text

elements.text: CHOICERenovation Mortgage secured by a property previously owned free and clear by
elements.label: text

elements.text: the Borrower is considered a “no cash-out” refinance Mortgage if the proceeds are used
elements.label: text

elements.text: only to finance the eligible renovations described in Section 4607.6.
elements.label: text

elements.text: At least one Borrower must have been on the title to the subject property for at least six
elements.label: text

elements.text: months prior to the Note Date unless one of the exceptions to this title requirement that
elements.label: text

elements.text: are specified in the cash-out refinance Mortgage requirements of Section 4301.5(b)
elements.label: text

elements.text: applies.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (c) Maximum LTV/TLTV/HTLTV ratios
elements.label: text

elements.text: Each CHOICERenovation Mortgage must have maximum LTV/TLTV/HTLTV ratios that
elements.label: text

elements.text: comply with the LTV/TLTV/HTLTV ratios in Section 4203.1(b) and any other applicable
elements.label: text

elements.text: LTV/TLTV/HTLTV ratio requirement for the specific Mortgage Product or offering.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (d) Determining value
elements.label: text

elements.text: The value used to determine the LTV, TLTV and HTLTV ratios must be established as
elements.label: text

elements.text: follows:
elements.label: text

elements.text: Purchase transaction “No cash-out” refinance
elements.label: text

elements.text: Value is the lesser of:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ The purchase price of the Mortgaged
elements.label: text

elements.text: Premises prior to the renovations plus
elements.label: text

elements.text: the total renovation costs, or
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Appraised value of the Mortgaged
elements.label: text

elements.text: Premises, as completed
elements.label: text

elements.text: Value is the appraised value of the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Mortgaged Premises, as completed.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (e) Secondary financing
elements.label: text

elements.text: Secondary financing must comply with Guide requirements for the specific Mortgage
elements.label: text

elements.text: Product or offering.
elements.label: text

elements.text: 4607.5: Underwriting CHOICERenovation® Mortgages
elements.label: section_header

elements.text: This section contains requirements related to:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Residential loan application
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Uniform Underwriting and Transmittal Summary
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Accept Mortgages
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Temporary rental payments for Mortgages with Settlement Dates before completion of
elements.label: text

elements.text: renovations
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Rental income from the subject property
elements.label: text

elements.text: (a) Residential loan application
elements.label: text

elements.text: The Seller must code the Purpose of Loan on Form 65, Uniform Residential Loan
elements.label: text

elements.text: Application, as either Purchase or Refinance (as applicable) and not as Construction or
elements.label: text

elements.text: Construction-Permanent.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (b) Uniform Underwriting and Transmittal Summary
elements.label: text

elements.text: The Seller must code the Loan Purpose on Form 1077, Uniform Underwriting and
elements.label: text

elements.text: Transmittal Summary, as either Purchase or No Cash-Out Refinance (as applicable) and not
elements.label: text

elements.text: as Home Improvement. The Seller must indicate in the Underwriter Comments if the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Mortgage is a CHOICERenovation® Mortgage.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (c) Accept Mortgages
elements.label: text

elements.text: All CHOICERenovation Mortgages must be Accept Mortgages.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (d) Temporary rental payments for Mortgages with Settlement Dates before completion of
elements.label: text

elements.text: renovations
elements.label: text

elements.text: For CHOICERenovation In Progress and CHOICEReno eXPress Mortgages secured by
elements.label: text

elements.text: Primary Residences, if the Borrower plans to rent another property as their principal domicile
elements.label: text

elements.text: during the renovations, the rental lease must be documented in the Mortgage file.
elements.label: text

elements.text: The temporary rental payment for the Borrower’s principal domicile must be included in the
elements.label: text

elements.text: calculation of the monthly debt payment-to-income ratio if:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ More than 10 payments remain on the lease term as of the Note Date, or
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ The rental lease has a renewal or extension option
elements.label: text

elements.text: (e) Rental income from the subject property
elements.label: text

elements.text: For a CHOICERenovation Mortgage secured by a 1- to 4-unit Primary Residence or a 1-unit
elements.label: text

elements.text: Investment Property, rental income from the unit(s), including ADU(s), that are included in
elements.label: text

elements.text: the renovation project must not be used to qualify the Borrower.
elements.label: text

elements.text: 4607.6: Eligible renovations for CHOICERenovation®
elements.label: section_header

elements.text: Mortgages (06/04/25)
elements.label: text

elements.text: This section contains requirements related to:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Eligible and ineligible uses of Mortgage proceeds
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Compliance with applicable laws and project documents
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Renovations to Manufactured Homes
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Payoff of short-term financing that financed renovations completed prior to the Note Date
elements.label: text

elements.text: (a) Eligible and ineligible uses of Mortgage proceeds
elements.label: text

elements.text: CHOICERenovation® Mortgage proceeds must only be used to finance renovations that are
elements.label: text

elements.text: made to a property with an existing dwelling and may include:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Fees related to plans and specifications, permits, title updates, appraisals, draw
elements.label: text

elements.text: inspections and the final inspection
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ An amount up to, but no more than, six monthly payments of principal, interest, taxes and
elements.label: text

elements.text: insurance
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Contingency reserve funds as described in Section 4607.11(b)
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ The payoff of short-term financing that provided the Borrower with funds to repair,
elements.label: text

elements.text: restore, rehabilitate or renovate an existing home as described in Section 4607.6(d)
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Adding or renovating outdoor structures used for leisure and recreation, including, but
elements.label: text

elements.text: not limited to, swimming pools, decking, screening and porch and patio additions
elements.label: text

elements.text: Note: In accordance with Section 4607.11(b), CHOICERenovation Mortgages whose
elements.label: text

elements.text: proceeds are used exclusively to finance the addition or renovation of outdoor structures
elements.label: text

elements.text: used for leisure and recreation will not be subject to the minimum contingency reserve
elements.label: text

elements.text: requirements.
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Renovations or repairs to a property that has been damaged in a disaster or for
elements.label: text

elements.text: renovations that will protect the Mortgaged Premises in case of a future disaster (e.g.,
elements.label: text

elements.text: storm surge barriers, foundation retrofitting for earthquakes, retaining walls, etc.)
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Renovations to improve the energy and/or water efficiency, the health and safety and/or
elements.label: text

elements.text: the resiliency and preventiveness of the Mortgaged Premises
elements.label: text

elements.text: Note: See Section 4607.16(b) for special requirements when CHOICERenovation
elements.label: text

elements.text: Mortgages are combined with GreenCHOICE Mortgages® and the eligibility for the
elements.label: text

elements.text: credit for Credit Fees for GreenCHOICE Mortgages
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Adding or renovating an ADU, including a Manufactured Home ADU
elements.label: text

elements.text: Note: See Section 4607.6(d) below regarding the payoff of short-term financing that
elements.label: text

elements.text: financed the addition or renovation of an ADU prior to the Note Date.
elements.label: text

elements.text: CHOICERenovation® Mortgage proceeds may not be used:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ To raze an existing structure and build a new primary dwelling unit
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ For personal property with the exception of new appliances
elements.label: text

elements.text: (b) Compliance with applicable laws and project documents
elements.label: text

elements.text: Renovations must comply with all applicable State and local laws and regulations, including
elements.label: text

elements.text: zoning regulations. All required permits and approvals must be obtained.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Renovations of properties located in Planned Unit Developments (PUDs), Condominium
elements.label: text

elements.text: Projects or Cooperative Projects must comply with all applicable project conditions,
elements.label: text

elements.text: covenants and restrictions.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (c) Renovations to Manufactured Homes
elements.label: text

elements.text: Renovations to a primary dwelling unit that is a Manufactured Home or a Manufactured
elements.label: text

elements.text: Home ADU are allowed, provided the Manufactured Home remains in compliance with
elements.label: text

elements.text: HUD’s property acceptability criteria for Manufactured Homes and the general requirements
elements.label: text

elements.text: in Section 5703.2, property eligibility requirements for Manufactured Homes in Section
elements.label: text

elements.text: 5703.3(a) and requirements for eligible properties with an ADU in Section 5601.2, as
elements.label: text

elements.text: applicable.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Renovations may include the removal of a Manufactured Home on the property that is not
elements.label: text

elements.text: the primary dwelling unit, provided any effect of its removal on the value of the Mortgaged
elements.label: text

elements.text: Premises is reported by the appraiser when determining the “as completed” value of the
elements.label: text

elements.text: property pursuant to the appraisal requirements for CHOICERenovation Mortgages in
elements.label: text

elements.text: Section 4607.8.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (d) Payoff of short-term financing that financed renovations completed prior to the Note
elements.label: text

elements.text: Date
elements.label: text

elements.text: For CHOICERenovation Mortgages that are “no cash-out” refinance Mortgages, proceeds
elements.label: text

elements.text: can be used to pay off short-term financing that financed renovations (including, but not
elements.label: text

elements.text: limited to, the addition or renovation of an ADU) completed prior to the Note Date, provided
elements.label: text

elements.text: the following requirements are met:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ The CHOICERenovation Mortgage is not secured by a Manufactured Home
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ The CHOICERenovation Mortgage is not a CHOICEReno eXPress® Mortgage
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ The Borrower on the CHOICERenovation Mortgage must be the Borrower on, and
elements.label: text

elements.text: obligated to repay, the short-term financing.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Exceptions:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ❑ A Borrower may be omitted in the event of death or divorce, or
elements.label: text

elements.text: ❑ A Borrower who is a Related Person may be added, provided that all Borrowers on
elements.label: text

elements.text: the CHOICERenovation Mortgage are owner-occupants of the Mortgaged Premises
elements.label: text

elements.text: and considered in the underwriting of the CHOICERenovation Mortgage
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ All renovations financed by the short-term financing must be completed prior to the Note
elements.label: text

elements.text: Date of the CHOICERenovation Mortgage with no outstanding obligations related to
elements.label: text

elements.text: such financing
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ All renovations financed by the short-term financing must be completed prior to the
elements.label: text

elements.text: appraisal, which must reflect these renovations having been made. The appraisal report
elements.label: text

elements.text: obtained in accordance with CHOICERenovation Mortgage appraisal requirements in
elements.label: text

elements.text: Section 4607.8 must only be completed subject to completion of any proposed
elements.label: text

elements.text: renovations also being financed with the CHOICERenovation Mortgage.
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ The Mortgage file must contain copies of all relevant documentation, including, but not
elements.label: text

elements.text: limited to:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ❑ The short-term financing agreement
elements.label: text

elements.text: ❑ Sufficient documentation (e.g., purchase contracts, plans and specifications, receipts,
elements.label: text

elements.text: invoices, lien waivers, etc.) on which to validate the actual cost of all renovations
elements.label: text

elements.text: financed by the short-term financing
elements.label: text

elements.text: ❑ A document clearly showing the calculation of the short-term financing
elements.label: text

elements.text: ❑ The Settlement/Closing Disclosure Statement or an alternative form required by law
elements.label: text

elements.text: for the closing of the short-term financing
elements.label: text

elements.text: ❑ The payoff statement
elements.label: text

elements.text: 4607.8: Appraisal requirements for
elements.label: section_header

elements.text: CHOICERenovation® Mortgages (01/26/26)
elements.label: text

elements.text: Refer to Bulletin 2025-7, which announced the policy requirements for Uniform Appraisal
elements.label: text

elements.text: Dataset (UAD) 3.6. Sellers may submit to the Uniform Collateral Data Portal® appraisal
elements.label: text

elements.text: reports that use UAD 3.6 before the mandatory effective November 2, 2026 version of this
elements.label: text

elements.text: section.
elements.label: text

elements.text: This section contains requirements related to:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Initial appraisal report
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Changes to original plans and specifications
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Final inspection and completion report
elements.label: text

elements.text: (a) Initial appraisal report
elements.label: text

elements.text: The Seller must obtain an appraisal report based on an interior and exterior inspection. The
elements.label: text

elements.text: appraisal report must include an “as completed” value of the subject property. If there are
elements.label: text

elements.text: proposed renovations being financed with the CHOICERenovation® Mortgage, the appraisal
elements.label: text

elements.text: report must be completed subject to completion of the proposed renovations.
elements.label: text

elements.text: The Seller must provide the appraiser with the cost estimates, plans and specifications for the
elements.label: text

elements.text: renovations.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Note: If Form 1000, Single Family Comparable Rent Schedule, or Form 72, Small
elements.label: text

elements.text: Residential Income Property Appraisal Report, is required, it must be completed using
elements.label: text

elements.text: current rental income for the subject property. See Section 5306.1.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (b) Changes to original plans and specifications
elements.label: text

elements.text: If, after the appraiser provides the “as completed” value, changes are made to the original
elements.label: text

elements.text: plans and specifications in accordance with the Seller responsibilities outlined in Section
elements.label: text

elements.text: 4607.9(a)(2), the Seller must notify the appraiser of the changes and provide change
elements.label: text

elements.text: documentation to the appraiser.
elements.label: text

elements.text: The appraiser must provide a new appraisal to reflect the changes and account for the impact
elements.label: text

elements.text: on the “as completed” value.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (c) Final inspection and completion report
elements.label: text

elements.text: Upon completion of all proposed renovations, the appraiser must perform a final inspection
elements.label: text

elements.text: of the property and complete a completion report in accordance with the requirements in
elements.label: text

elements.text: Section 5605.8 for verifying completion.
elements.label: text

elements.text: The completion report must document that all renovations were completed in accordance
elements.label: text

elements.text: with the plans and specifications and must include photographs of the completed renovations.
elements.label: text

elements.text: 4607.8: Appraisal requirements for
elements.label: section_header

elements.text: CHOICERenovation® Mortgages (Future effective date
elements.label: text

elements.text: This section contains requirements related to:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Initial appraisal report
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Changes to original plans and specifications
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Final inspection and completion report
elements.label: text

elements.text: (a) Initial appraisal report
elements.label: text

elements.text: The Seller must obtain a Traditional Appraisal Report that includes an “as completed” value
elements.label: text

elements.text: of the subject property. If there are proposed renovations being financed with the
elements.label: text

elements.text: CHOICERenovation® Mortgage, the appraisal report must be completed subject to
elements.label: text

elements.text: completion of the proposed renovations.
elements.label: text

elements.text: The Seller must provide the appraiser with the cost estimates, plans and specifications for the
elements.label: text

elements.text: renovations.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (b) Changes to original plans and specifications
elements.label: text

elements.text: If, after the appraiser provides the “as completed” value, changes are made to the original
elements.label: text

elements.text: plans and specifications in accordance with the Seller responsibilities outlined in Section
elements.label: text

elements.text: 4607.9(a)(2), the Seller must notify the appraiser of the changes and provide change
elements.label: text

elements.text: documentation to the appraiser.
elements.label: text

elements.text: The appraiser must provide a new appraisal report to reflect the changes and account for the
elements.label: text

elements.text: impact on the “as completed” value.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (c) Final inspection and completion report
elements.label: text

elements.text: Upon completion of all proposed renovations, the appraiser must perform a final inspection
elements.label: text

elements.text: of the property and complete a completion report in accordance with the requirements in
elements.label: text

elements.text: Section 5605.9 for verifying completion.
elements.label: text

elements.text: The completion report must document that all renovations were completed in accordance
elements.label: text

elements.text: with the plans and specifications and must include photographs of the completed renovations.
elements.label: text

elements.text: 4607.9: Seller responsibilities for renovation work and
elements.label: section_header

elements.text: process for CHOICERenovation® Mortgages (11/25/24)
elements.label: text

elements.text: This section contains requirements related to:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Responsibilities during the renovation period
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Other Seller responsibilities
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Notification of completion of the subject property
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Seller responsibilities for third parties
elements.label: text

elements.text: (a) Responsibilities during the renovation period
elements.label: text

elements.text: During the renovation period:
elements.label: text

elements.text: 1. The Seller is responsible for managing the Custodial Account for Renovation Funds as
elements.label: text

elements.text: described in Section 4607.12 and corresponding draws
elements.label: text

elements.text: 2. The Seller must manage any changes to the plans and specifications requested by the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Borrower during the course of renovations.
elements.label: text

elements.text: If any changes are made to the plans and specifications and/or the estimated time of
elements.label: text

elements.text: completion for the renovations, the changes must be agreed upon via a change order
elements.label: text

elements.text: signed by the Borrower and the contractor and approved by the Seller. Documentation
elements.label: text

elements.text: evidencing the change order must be signed by the Borrower and the contractor and must
elements.label: text

elements.text: include the following, as applicable:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Detailed description of the changes
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Updated itemized renovation costs
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Updated total cost of the renovations
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Any changes to the estimated Completion Date
elements.label: text

elements.text: 3. The Seller may not approve changes to the plans and specifications if such changes
elements.label: text

elements.text: impact the loan-to-value (LTV)/total LTV (TLTV)/Home Equity Line of Credit
elements.label: text

elements.text: (HELOC) TLTV (HTLTV) ratio or the property such that the Mortgage:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Would not have been eligible for sale under the terms of the Seller’s Purchase
elements.label: text

elements.text: Documents in effect on the Settlement Date, or
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Would have been eligible for sale but under different terms
elements.label: text

elements.text: (b) Other Seller responsibilities
elements.label: text

elements.text: The Seller is responsible for compliance with the following requirements:
elements.label: text

elements.text: 1. The Mortgage file must contain all relevant documentation, including, but not limited to,
elements.label: text

elements.text: copies of:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Costs of the renovations
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Purchase contracts
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Bids
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Appraisal(s)
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Renovation contract(s)
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Plans and specifications
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Permits and applicable homeowners association approvals
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Documents related to change orders and draws
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Completion report
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Close-out documentation for the Custodial Account for Renovation Funds
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Title updates
elements.label: text

elements.text: Note: See Section 4607.6(d) regarding additional Mortgage file documentation
elements.label: text

elements.text: requirements when CHOICERenovation® Mortgage proceeds are used to pay off short￾term financing that provided the Borrower with funds to repair, restore, rehabilitate or
elements.label: text

elements.text: renovate an existing home.
elements.label: text

elements.text: 2. The Seller must notify Freddie Mac through Loan Status Hub® if there are any concerns
elements.label: text

elements.text: that the renovations will not be completed by the Completion Date or if there are any
elements.label: text

elements.text: changes that impact the “as completed” value of the property as determined by the
elements.label: text

elements.text: appraiser.
elements.label: text

elements.text: If a Completion Date extension is necessary, the Seller must follow the requirements in
elements.label: text

elements.text: Section 4607.4(a)(ii) to request an extension.
elements.label: text

elements.text: 3. Unless otherwise noted below, all contractor(s) and/or tradespersons chosen by the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Borrower to complete the renovations must:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Have entered into an executed, binding renovation contract with the Borrower to
elements.label: text

elements.text: complete the renovations no later than the Completion Date. The contract must
elements.label: text

elements.text: include an indemnification provision requiring the contractor to indemnify the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Borrower for any property loss or damage caused by the contractor, its employees or
elements.label: text

elements.text: its subcontractors.
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Be licensed and insured as required by local and/or State requirements, and
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Be financially able to perform the duties necessary to complete the renovation work
elements.label: text

elements.text: in a timely manner
elements.label: text

elements.text: The above requirements do not apply if the Borrower, acting as general contractor,
elements.label: text

elements.text: performs all the work in accordance with Section 4607.10(a) or if renovations are
elements.label: text

elements.text: purchased from a home improvement store in accordance with Section 4607.10(c).
elements.label: text

elements.text: 4. The Seller must obtain title updates as necessary to ensure that the CHOICERenovation
elements.label: text

elements.text: Mortgage meets all title insurance requirements in Chapter 4702
elements.label: text

elements.text: 5. After all renovations are completed, the CHOICERenovation Mortgage must remain a
elements.label: text

elements.text: valid First Lien in accordance with Section 4201.2
elements.label: text

elements.text: (c) Notification of completion of the subject property
elements.label: text

elements.text: For CHOICERenovation In Progress and CHOICEReno eXPress Mortgages, when the
elements.label: text

elements.text: renovations are complete, the Seller must notify Freddie Mac through Loan Status Hub and
elements.label: text

elements.text: provide the completion report including photographs of the completed renovations.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Until the Seller receives written acknowledgment from Freddie Mac through Loan Status
elements.label: text

elements.text: Hub that all renovations have been completed to satisfy the “as completed” appraised value
elements.label: text

elements.text: of the original appraisal and Freddie Mac approves the removal of recourse, the Seller
elements.label: text

elements.text: remains responsible for the requirements of Section 4607.9.
elements.label: text

elements.text: The Seller represents and warrants that, as of the date the Seller notifies Freddie Mac that all
elements.label: text

elements.text: renovations are complete, all requirements in this Chapter 4607 have been met.
elements.label: text

elements.text: If notice that all renovations are complete is not received by Freddie Mac by the Completion
elements.label: text

elements.text: Date, the Mortgage will be subject to a repurchase.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (d) Seller responsibilities for third parties
elements.label: text

elements.text: A third party may perform all or some of the processing, management and performance of
elements.label: text

elements.text: draw inspections and/or maintenance and management of disbursements from the completion
elements.label: text

elements.text: escrow account or Custodial Account for Renovation Funds as described in Sections 4607.11
elements.label: text

elements.text: and 4607.12, as applicable, and other requirements described above as responsibilities or
elements.label: text

elements.text: obligations of the Seller.
elements.label: text

elements.text: A third party may not open and perform ongoing maintenance of the Custodial Account for
elements.label: text

elements.text: Renovation Funds except as otherwise provided for in Section 4607.17(a) for Transfers of
elements.label: text

elements.text: Servicing of CHOICERenovation Mortgages.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Although these functions may not be performed directly by the Seller, the Seller is
elements.label: text

elements.text: responsible for the accuracy and integrity of the information provided by the third party and
elements.label: text

elements.text: for compliance with these and all requirements in the Seller’s Purchase Documents.
elements.label: text

elements.text: 4607.10: Contractor requirements and advancing the
elements.label: section_header

elements.text: costs of materials for CHOICERenovation® Mortgages
elements.label: text

elements.text: This section contains requirements related to:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Borrower as contractor
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Contractors and/or tradespersons chosen by the Borrower
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Renovations purchased from a home improvement store
elements.label: text

elements.text: (a) Borrower as contractor
elements.label: text

elements.text: The Borrower may act as the general contractor and/or may perform some or all of the work
elements.label: text

elements.text: to complete the renovations when the following requirements are met:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ The Borrower must be a licensed contractor when required by applicable local and/or
elements.label: text

elements.text: State law
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ The Borrower must be insured to perform the renovations on the subject property
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ The Borrower must be qualified to complete the renovations
elements.label: text

elements.text: The Borrower must submit to the Seller a plan detailing the work items the Borrower will
elements.label: text

elements.text: perform.
elements.label: text

elements.text: CHOICERenovation® Mortgage proceeds may be used to reimburse the Borrower for the
elements.label: text

elements.text: cost of materials, but may not be used to reimburse the Borrower for labor costs.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (b) Contractor(s) and/or tradespersons chosen by the Borrower
elements.label: text

elements.text: Contractor(s) and/or tradespersons chosen by the Borrower to complete the renovations must
elements.label: text

elements.text: meet the following requirements in accordance with Section 4607.9(b)(3):
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ The contractor(s) and/or tradespersons entered into an executed, binding renovation
elements.label: text

elements.text: contract with the Borrower to complete the renovations no later than the Completion
elements.label: text

elements.text: Date. The contract must include an indemnification provision requiring the contractor to
elements.label: text

elements.text: indemnify the Borrower for any property loss or damage caused by the contractor, its
elements.label: text

elements.text: employees or its subcontractors.
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ The contractor(s) and/or tradespersons must be licensed and insured as required by local
elements.label: text

elements.text: and/or State requirements
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ The contractor(s) and/or tradespersons must be financially able to perform the duties
elements.label: text

elements.text: necessary to complete the renovation work in a timely manner
elements.label: text

elements.text: Except when the Borrower is acting as the contractor and/or tradesperson, up to 50% of the
elements.label: text

elements.text: cost of materials may be advanced to the contractor(s) and/or tradespersons at closing in lieu
elements.label: text

elements.text: of such funds being deposited into the completion escrow account or Custodial Account for
elements.label: text

elements.text: Renovation Funds (as described in Sections 4607.11 and 4607.12), as applicable, when the
elements.label: text

elements.text: above requirements are met.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (c) Renovations purchased from a home improvement store
elements.label: text

elements.text: If the Borrower chooses a home improvement store to have the renovation work completed
elements.label: text

elements.text: and the home improvement store’s renovation program requires payment-in-full at the point
elements.label: text

elements.text: of purchase, renovations may be purchased from the home improvement store at closing,
elements.label: text

elements.text: provided the following requirements are met:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ The CHOICERenovation Mortgage is a “no cash-out” refinance Mortgage
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ The Seller must review the home improvement store’s renovation program to determine
elements.label: text

elements.text: that the following requirements are met:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ❑ The home improvement store is financially able to perform the duties necessary to
elements.label: text

elements.text: have the renovation work completed in a timely manner and pay the contractor(s)
elements.label: text

elements.text: and/or tradespersons chosen by the home improvement store to complete the
elements.label: text

elements.text: renovations. A contractor and/or tradesperson may not require payment directly from
elements.label: text

elements.text: the Borrower.
elements.label: text

elements.text: ❑ The home improvement store has a robust contractor approval process that is
elements.label: text

elements.text: managed, maintained and updated regularly
elements.label: text

elements.text: ❑ The contractor(s) and/or tradespersons chosen by the home improvement store to
elements.label: text

elements.text: complete the renovations is licensed and insured as required by local and/or State
elements.label: text

elements.text: requirements, and they must be approved under the home improvement store’s
elements.label: text

elements.text: contractor approval process during the course of renovations.
elements.label: text

elements.text: In the event a contractor and/or tradesperson becomes unapproved or is unable to
elements.label: text

elements.text: complete the renovation work in a timely manner, the home improvement store must
elements.label: text

elements.text: choose other approved contractor(s) and/or tradespersons to complete the renovations.
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ The Borrower may not be chosen by the home improvement store to complete the
elements.label: text

elements.text: renovations, even if the Borrower is a licensed contractor and/or is licensed and qualified
elements.label: text

elements.text: to complete the renovations
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ The home improvement store must have entered into an executed, binding renovation
elements.label: text

elements.text: contract with the Borrower to complete the renovations no later than the Completion
elements.label: text

elements.text: Date. The contract must include an indemnification provision requiring the home
elements.label: text

elements.text: improvement store to indemnify the Borrower for any property loss or damage caused by
elements.label: text

elements.text: the contractor(s) and/or tradespersons chosen by the home improvement store to
elements.label: text

elements.text: complete the renovations.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Up to 100% of the cost of materials and renovation costs identified in the renovation contract
elements.label: text

elements.text: (including labor costs) may be paid to a home improvement store at closing in lieu of such
elements.label: text

elements.text: funds being deposited into the completion escrow account or Custodial Account for
elements.label: text

elements.text: Renovation Funds (as described in Sections 4607.11 and 4607.12), as applicable, if the
elements.label: text

elements.text: requirements above are met.
elements.label: text

elements.text: 4607.11: Funds for renovations and contingency
elements.label: section_header

elements.text: reserve requirements for CHOICERenovation®
elements.label: text

elements.text: Mortgages (06/03/26)
elements.label: text

elements.text: This section contains requirements related to:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Renovation funds
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Contingency reserve
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Unused funds
elements.label: text

elements.text: (a) Renovation funds
elements.label: text

elements.text: On the Note Date, the renovation funds (sufficient to cover the total cost of the renovations
elements.label: text

elements.text: minus any advances for the cost of materials and/or renovation costs paid pursuant to Section
elements.label: text

elements.text: 4607.10) must be deposited into a completion escrow account. The Seller and the Borrower
elements.label: text

elements.text: must execute a written escrow agreement detailing how the funds will be managed and
elements.label: text

elements.text: disbursed. A copy of the escrow agreement must be retained in the Mortgage file.
elements.label: text

elements.text: For CHOICERenovation® In Progress Mortgages, the renovation funds must be deposited
elements.label: text

elements.text: into a Custodial Account for Renovation Funds, as described in Section 4607.12, on or
elements.label: text

elements.text: before the Settlement Date.
elements.label: text

elements.text: After taking into consideration any advances for the cost of materials and/or renovation costs
elements.label: text

elements.text: paid pursuant to Section 4607.10, the renovation costs identified in the renovation contract(s)
elements.label: text

elements.text: must be consistent with the amount of renovation funds deposited into the completion escrow
elements.label: text

elements.text: account or Custodial Account for Renovation Funds, as applicable.
elements.label: text

elements.text: If the CHOICERenovation Mortgage proceeds are insufficient to cover the contracted cost of
elements.label: text

elements.text: the renovations, the Borrower must deposit sufficient funds to pay the remaining amount into
elements.label: text

elements.text: the completion escrow account or Custodial Account for Renovation Funds, as applicable.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (b) Contingency reserve
elements.label: text

elements.text: In addition to the renovation funds required to be deposited into the completion escrow
elements.label: text

elements.text: account or Custodial Account for Renovation Funds, as applicable, the Seller must also
elements.label: text

elements.text: deposit a contingency reserve to cover unforeseen renovation costs. Contingency reserve
elements.label: text

elements.text: funds may come from the CHOICERenovation Mortgage proceeds or directly from the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Borrower.
elements.label: text

elements.text: A contingency reserve is not required for CHOICERenovation Mortgages whose proceeds
elements.label: text

elements.text: are used exclusively to finance the addition or renovation of outdoor structures used for
elements.label: text

elements.text: leisure and recreation. (See Section 4607.6 for a description of eligible renovations.)
elements.label: text

elements.text: (i) Minimum contingency reserve
elements.label: text

elements.text: For CHOICERenovation In Progress Mortgages, the minimum contingency reserve
elements.label: text

elements.text: amount must be greater than or equal to 10% of the total renovation costs identified in
elements.label: text

elements.text: the renovation contract(s), except that if the property utilities are not operable as
elements.label: text

elements.text: referenced in the renovation contract(s) and/or plans and specifications, the minimum
elements.label: text

elements.text: contingency reserve amount must be greater than or equal to 15% of the total
elements.label: text

elements.text: renovation costs identified in the renovation contract(s).
elements.label: text

elements.text: (ii) Maximum contingency reserve
elements.label: text

elements.text: For CHOICERenovation Mortgages, the contingency reserve amount must be less than
elements.label: text

elements.text: or equal to 20% of the total renovation costs identified in the renovation contract(s).
elements.label: text

elements.text: While a contingency reserve is not required for CHOICEReno eXPress® Mortgages or
elements.label: text

elements.text: CHOICERenovation Mortgages whose proceeds are used exclusively to finance the
elements.label: text

elements.text: addition or renovation of outdoor structures used for leisure and recreation, if the Seller
elements.label: text

elements.text: chooses to establish a contingency reserve, the contingency reserve amount must be
elements.label: text

elements.text: less than or equal to 20% of the total renovation costs identified in the renovation
elements.label: text

elements.text: contract(s).
elements.label: text

elements.text: (c) Unused funds
elements.label: text

elements.text: (i) CHOICERenovation Mortgage is current
elements.label: text

elements.text: If the CHOICERenovation Mortgage is current, any unused funds remaining in the
elements.label: text

elements.text: completion escrow account or Custodial Account for Renovation Funds, as applicable,
elements.label: text

elements.text: after the costs of all renovations have been paid to the appropriate parties must be used
elements.label: text

elements.text: for the following:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Reduce the UPB
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Complete additional renovations, provided the Seller must:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ❑ Document that additional renovations were paid for from the completion escrow
elements.label: text

elements.text: account or Custodial Account for Renovation Funds, as applicable, and verify the
elements.label: text

elements.text: funds are being used to further improve the Mortgaged Premises, and
elements.label: text

elements.text: ❑ Verify the additional renovation work has been completed by obtaining a
elements.label: text

elements.text: completion report pursuant to the appraisal requirements in Section 4607.8
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Reimburse Borrower contributed funds to the contingency reserve
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ If a “no cash-out” refinance Mortgage, disburse to the Borrower the remaining
elements.label: text

elements.text: proceeds, provided the total amount disbursed to the Borrower at closing and from
elements.label: text

elements.text: the unused funds does not exceed the maximum amount allowed under Section
elements.label: text

elements.text: 4301.4 for “no cash-out” refinance Mortgages
elements.label: text

elements.text: (ii) CHOICERenovation Mortgage is delinquent
elements.label: text

elements.text: If the CHOICERenovation Mortgage is delinquent, any unused funds remaining in the
elements.label: text

elements.text: completion escrow account or Custodial Account for Renovation Funds, as applicable,
elements.label: text

elements.text: after the costs of all renovations have been paid to the appropriate parties must be
elements.label: text

elements.text: applied in accordance with the application of payment requirements in the Note and
elements.label: text

elements.text: Security Instrument. After the CHOICERenovation Mortgage is brought current, any
elements.label: text

elements.text: remaining unused funds (including contingency reserve funds not provided by the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Borrower) must be used for the following:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Reduce the UPB
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Complete additional renovations, provided the Seller must:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ❑ Document that additional renovations were paid for from the completion escrow
elements.label: text

elements.text: account or Custodial Account for Renovation Funds, as applicable, and verify
elements.label: text

elements.text: the funds are being used to further improve the Mortgaged Premises, and
elements.label: text

elements.text: ❑ Verify the additional renovation work has been completed by obtaining a
elements.label: text

elements.text: completion report pursuant to the appraisal requirements in Section 4607.8
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Reimburse Borrower contributed funds to the contingency reserve
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ If a “no cash-out” refinance Mortgage, disburse to the Borrower the remaining
elements.label: text

elements.text: proceeds, provided the total amount disbursed to the Borrower at closing and from
elements.label: text

elements.text: the unused funds does not exceed the maximum amount allowed under Section
elements.label: text

elements.text: 4301.4 for “no cash-out” refinance Mortgages
elements.label: text

elements.text: 4607.12: Custodial Accounts for Renovation Funds for
elements.label: section_header

elements.text: CHOICERenovation® Mortgages (09/10/25)
elements.label: text

elements.text: This section contains requirements related to:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ General requirements
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Opening the Custodial Account for Renovation Funds
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Changing or transferring Custodial Accounts for Renovation Funds
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Freddie Mac’s rights
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Seller responsibilities related to maintaining the Custodial Account
elements.label: text

elements.text: The requirements of this section apply to CHOICERenovation® In Progress Mortgages.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (a) General requirements
elements.label: text

elements.text: For each Seller/Servicer number associated with one or more CHOICERenovation
elements.label: text

elements.text: Mortgage(s), the Seller must:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Open and maintain a separate completion escrow account solely to retain the
elements.label: text

elements.text: CHOICERenovation Mortgage proceeds and any other deposited funds to be used to
elements.label: text

elements.text: complete the renovations of Mortgaged Premises securing CHOICERenovation
elements.label: text

elements.text: Mortgages (such funds, the “Renovation Funds” and each such account, the “Custodial
elements.label: text

elements.text: Account for Renovation Funds”)
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Open and maintain each Custodial Account for Renovation Funds in accordance with the
elements.label: text

elements.text: requirements of this section and the account must be utilized exclusively for the
elements.label: text

elements.text: CHOICERenovation Mortgages it sells to Freddie Mac under a particular Seller/Servicer
elements.label: text

elements.text: number
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Ensure the Custodial Account for Renovation Funds does not carry a negative balance,
elements.label: text

elements.text: even if permitted by its depository institution
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Maintain a separate account record for each CHOICERenovation Mortgage’s Renovation
elements.label: text

elements.text: Funds retained in the Custodial Account for Renovation Funds until all appropriate
elements.label: text

elements.text: parties have been paid and the renovation work is completed and all unused funds have
elements.label: text

elements.text: been disbursed in accordance with Section 4607.11(c) for unused funds. Each separate
elements.label: text

elements.text: account record may not reflect a negative balance.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (b) Opening the Custodial Account for Renovation Funds
elements.label: text

elements.text: Each Custodial Account for Renovation Funds must meet the requirements in Section
elements.label: text

elements.text: 8302.1(b) for Custodial Accounts, Section 8302.2(b) for an Eligible Depository, Section
elements.label: text

elements.text: 8302.1(c)(iii) for Escrow Custodial Accounts and Section 8302.1(d) for Custodial Account
elements.label: text

elements.text: documentation.
elements.label: text

elements.text: The Seller must establish a separate Custodial Account for Renovation Funds which must be
elements.label: text

elements.text: named “Freddie Mac Escrow Completion Custodial Account” or “Freddie Mac Renovation
elements.label: text

elements.text: Funds Custodial Account.”
elements.label: text

elements.text: With respect to the Escrow Custodial Account requirements in Section 8302.1(c)(iii), the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Seller may not:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Designate the Custodial Account for Renovation Funds using the abbreviated designation
elements.label: text

elements.text: “Freddie Mac Escrow Custodial Account”
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Commingle Renovation Funds with Escrow Funds in the same Custodial Account
elements.label: text

elements.text: Custodial Accounts for Renovation Funds are not subject to the tier rating requirements in
elements.label: text

elements.text: Section 8302.2(c).
elements.label: text

elements.text: (c) Changing or transferring Custodial Accounts for Renovation Funds
elements.label: text

elements.text: See Sections 8304.3(b) through 8304.3(d) for Freddie Mac’s requirements for changing or
elements.label: text

elements.text: transferring established Custodial Accounts, including Custodial Accounts for Renovation
elements.label: text

elements.text: Funds.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (d) Freddie Mac’s rights
elements.label: text

elements.text: Freddie Mac reserves the right to request that the Seller submit copies of Custodial Account
elements.label: text

elements.text: for Renovation Funds records, such as bank account statements, detailed trial balances and
elements.label: text

elements.text: completed reconciliations, variance logs, and supporting documentation for such records.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (e) Seller responsibilities related to maintaining the Custodial Account
elements.label: text

elements.text: The Seller must manage Custodial Accounts for Renovation Funds as required in Sections
elements.label: text

elements.text: 4607.13: Property insurance for CHOICERenovation®
elements.label: section_header

elements.text: Mortgages (10/02/24)
elements.label: text

elements.text: CHOICERenovation® Mortgages must meet the property insurance requirements in Chapter
elements.label: text

elements.text: 4703. Insurance coverage may need to be adjusted during the course of renovations and/or upon
elements.label: text

elements.text: completion of all renovations based on the “as completed” value of the property (as determined
elements.label: text

elements.text: by the appraiser pursuant to Section 4607.8) to meet the property insurance requirements in
elements.label: text

elements.text: Chapter 8202.
elements.label: text

elements.text: 4607.14: Recourse for CHOICERenovation® Mortgages
elements.label: section_header

elements.text: CHOICERenovation® In Progress Mortgages must be sold with recourse within the meaning of
elements.label: text

elements.text: Section 6201.2(b)(i), except that Freddie Mac may require the Seller to repurchase the
elements.label: text

elements.text: CHOICERenovation Mortgage if the Mortgage becomes 120 days delinquent while subject to
elements.label: text

elements.text: recourse.
elements.label: text

elements.text: The recourse will remain in full force and effect until:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ All renovations are completed,
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ The Seller requests removal of recourse in writing indicating the applicable Freddie Mac loan
elements.label: text

elements.text: number and provides the completion report, including photographs of the renovations, and
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Freddie Mac approves the removal of recourse, subject to the requirements below
elements.label: text

elements.text: In order to request removal of recourse the Seller must submit all required documentation
elements.label: text

elements.text: mentioned above to Freddie Mac through Loan Status Hub®, and the following requirements
elements.label: text

elements.text: must be met:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ There is no Delinquency at the time of the request
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ There has not been a Delinquency of 30 days more than once during the renovation period,
elements.label: text

elements.text: except that recourse may be removed at a later date once the Borrower has made 36
elements.label: text

elements.text: consecutive monthly payments with no Delinquencies
elements.label: text

elements.text: The Seller represents and warrants that, as of the date Freddie Mac approves the Seller’s request
elements.label: text

elements.text: for removal of recourse, all requirements in this Chapter 4607 have been met.
elements.label: text

elements.text: 4607.15: Representations and warranties for
elements.label: section_header

elements.text: CHOICERenovation® Mortgages (12/17/25)
elements.label: text

elements.text: The selling representation and warranty framework described in Section 1301.6 applies to
elements.label: text

elements.text: CHOICERenovation® Mortgages that meet the requirements of this Chapter 4607 provided that,
elements.label: text

elements.text: starting on the date that the payments are paid by the Borrower and not from the completion
elements.label: text

elements.text: escrow account or Custodial Account for Renovation Funds (as described in Sections 4607.11
elements.label: text

elements.text: and 4607.12), as applicable, the Borrower:
elements.label: text

elements.text: 1. Made the first 36 monthly payments due with no more than two 30-day Delinquencies and no
elements.label: text

elements.text: 60-day or greater Delinquencies, and
elements.label: text

elements.text: 2. Is not 30 or more days delinquent with respect to the 36th monthly payment
elements.label: text

elements.text: 4607.16: CHOICERenovation® Mortgages combined
elements.label: section_header

elements.text: with GreenCHOICE Mortgages® and credit for Credit
elements.label: text

elements.text: This section contains requirements related to:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Delivery requirements for CHOICERenovation® Mortgages
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Special requirements when CHOICERenovation Mortgages are combined with
elements.label: text

elements.text: GreenCHOICE Mortgages®
elements.label: text

elements.text: (a) Delivery requirements for CHOICERenovation Mortgages
elements.label: text

elements.text: See Section 6302.43 for special delivery requirements for CHOICERenovation Mortgages.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (b) Special requirements when CHOICERenovation Mortgages are combined with
elements.label: text

elements.text: GreenCHOICE Mortgages
elements.label: text

elements.text: When CHOICERenovation Mortgage proceeds finance renovations completed after the Note
elements.label: text

elements.text: Date to improve the energy and/or water efficiency, the health and safety and/or the
elements.label: text

elements.text: resiliency and preventiveness of the Mortgaged Premises, the CHOICERenovation Mortgage
elements.label: text

elements.text: may be eligible for the credit for Credit Fees for GreenCHOICE Mortgages (see Exhibit 19,
elements.label: text

elements.text: Credit Fees, and Exhibit 19A, Credit Fee Cap Eligibility Criteria), provided the following
elements.label: text

elements.text: requirements are met:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Except as stated below, the requirements in Section 4606.3(a) for GreenCHOICE
elements.label: text

elements.text: Mortgages must be met for the subject Mortgage. This includes, but is not limited to:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ❑ The cost of the financed renovations to improve the energy and/or water efficiency,
elements.label: text

elements.text: the health and safety and/or the resiliency and preventiveness of the Mortgaged
elements.label: text

elements.text: Premises must not exceed 15% of the “as completed” appraised value of the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Mortgaged Premises.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Note: In all cases, the maximum financed renovation costs must comply with the
elements.label: text

elements.text: requirements in Section 4607.7, as applicable.
elements.label: text

elements.text: ❑ Eligible improvements as described in Section 4606.1(b) with an aggregate cost of
elements.label: text

elements.text: less than or equal to $6,500 may be completed without obtaining an energy report. If
elements.label: text

elements.text: the aggregate cost should exceed $6,500, an energy report or alternative
elements.label: text

elements.text: documentation in lieu of an energy report meeting the requirements in Section 4606.4
elements.label: text

elements.text: must be obtained and retained in the Mortgage file.
elements.label: text

elements.text: ❑ The energy report exception stated in Section 4606.4(c), when proceeds are used to
elements.label: text

elements.text: finance renewable energy sources, may apply so long as the appraiser documents the
elements.label: text

elements.text: projected income by utilizing PV Value, Ei Value or a similar tool as referenced in
elements.label: text

elements.text: Section 5601.4, and that the cost effectiveness has been demonstrated
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ All renovations, including renovations to improve the energy and/or water efficiency, the
elements.label: text

elements.text: health and safety and/or the resiliency and preventiveness of the Mortgaged Premises,
elements.label: text

elements.text: must be completed by the Completion Date
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ The renovation funds are deposited into a completion escrow account or Custodial
elements.label: text

elements.text: Account for Renovation Funds (as described in Sections 4607.11 and 4607.12), as
elements.label: text

elements.text: applicable, and
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ If the CHOICERenovation Mortgage is current, any funds remaining in the completion
elements.label: text

elements.text: escrow account or Custodial Account for Renovation Funds, as applicable, after the costs
elements.label: text

elements.text: of all renovations have been paid to the appropriate parties either must be used to reduce
elements.label: text

elements.text: the UPB or may be used for additional renovations, as described in Section 4607.11(c)
elements.label: text

elements.text: For CHOICERenovation Mortgages that are eligible for the credit for Credit Fees for
elements.label: text

elements.text: GreenCHOICE Mortgages pursuant to this Section 4607.16(b), in addition to meeting the
elements.label: text

elements.text: special delivery requirements for CHOICERenovation Mortgages in Section 6302.43, the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Seller must deliver the ULDD Data Point(s) for GreenCHOICE Mortgages specified in
elements.label: text

elements.text: Section 6302.23.
elements.label: text

elements.text: 4607.17: Transfers of Servicing for
elements.label: section_header

elements.text: CHOICERenovation® Mortgages (09/10/25)
elements.label: text

elements.text: This section contains requirements related to:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Requirements for CHOICERenovation® In Progress Mortgages related to a Concurrent
elements.label: text

elements.text: Transfer of Servicing
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Prohibited Transfers of Servicing
elements.label: text

elements.text: (a) Requirements for CHOICERenovation In Progress Mortgages related to a Concurrent
elements.label: text

elements.text: Transfer of Servicing
elements.label: text

elements.text: For Sellers that have obtained Freddie Mac’s prior written approval, Concurrent Transfers of
elements.label: text

elements.text: Servicing involving CHOICERenovation In Progress Mortgages are permitted, provided the
elements.label: text

elements.text: following requirements are met. (See this Section 4607.17(b) regarding Subsequent Transfers
elements.label: text

elements.text: of Servicing involving such CHOICERenovation Mortgages and Transfers of Servicing
elements.label: text

elements.text: involving CHOICEReno eXPress® Mortgages.)
elements.label: text

elements.text: Consistent with Sections 7101.3(c) through 7101.3(f), the Seller, as Transferor Servicer,
elements.label: text

elements.text: remains jointly liable with the Transferee Servicer for all selling representation and warranty
elements.label: text

elements.text: obligations, including obligations in this Chapter 4607 related to the completion of the
elements.label: text

elements.text: renovations, even if such obligations are performed after the Effective Date of Transfer. This
elements.label: text

elements.text: includes, but is not limited to, ensuring completion of all renovations and delivering through
elements.label: text

elements.text: Loan Status Hub® the required notice and documentation to support completion of the
elements.label: text

elements.text: renovations in accordance with the Seller responsibilities in Section 4607.9(c) and fulfilling
elements.label: text

elements.text: the recourse obligation in the event that Freddie Mac does not approve removal of recourse
elements.label: text

elements.text: pursuant to Section 4607.14.
elements.label: text

elements.text: In addition to the requirements governing Concurrent Transfers of Servicing in the Guide, the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Transferor Servicer must inform the Transferee Servicer whether the Transfer of Servicing
elements.label: text

elements.text: involves CHOICERenovation In Progress Mortgages and must provide a list of such
elements.label: text

elements.text: Mortgages (identified, at a minimum, by Transferor Servicer loan number and Freddie Mac
elements.label: text

elements.text: loan number) to the Transferee Servicer.
elements.label: text

elements.text: A Transferee Servicer may engage an Outsourced Vendor or Servicing Agent, which may be
elements.label: text

elements.text: the Seller, to perform some or all of the activities set forth in Section 4607.9(d). The
elements.label: text

elements.text: Transferee Servicer must have in place appropriate processes and communication protocols
elements.label: text

elements.text: with any Outsourced Vendor or Servicing Agent it engages to perform the obligations in this
elements.label: text

elements.text: Chapter 4607 related to the completion of the renovations, including, but not limited to:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Requiring that any remaining funds in the Custodial Account for Renovation Funds (as
elements.label: text

elements.text: described in Section 4607.12) to be remitted to the Servicer to be applied to reduce the
elements.label: text

elements.text: UPB or returned to the Borrower, as applicable, in accordance with Section 4607.11(c).
elements.label: text

elements.text: The Servicer may outsource the opening and ongoing maintenance of the Custodial
elements.label: text

elements.text: Account for Renovation Funds only to its Servicing Agent, if applicable. An Outsourced
elements.label: text

elements.text: Vendor may be an authorized party to the account’s signature card and act as an agent of
elements.label: text

elements.text: the primary account holder (either the Servicer or Servicing Agent, as applicable).
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Notifying the Outsourced Vendor of any defaults related to the CHOICERenovation
elements.label: text

elements.text: Mortgage and ensuring appropriate actions are being taken, such as:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ❑ Relaying adverse information related to the Mortgaged Premises
elements.label: text

elements.text: ❑ If the CHOICERenovation Mortgage is delinquent, applying any unused funds in
elements.label: text

elements.text: accordance with the application of payment requirements in the Note and Security
elements.label: text

elements.text: Instrument (see Section 4607.11(c))
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Notifying Freddie Mac in accordance with Section 1301.2(b) and the Outsourced Vendor
elements.label: text

elements.text: if the Servicer or its Servicing Agent, if any, determines the Borrower was added to the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Office of Foreign Assets Control Specially Designated Nationals list
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Requiring any Mortgage proceeds allocated to make monthly payments of principal,
elements.label: text

elements.text: interest, taxes and insurance pursuant to Section 4607.6(a) to be remitted from the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Custodial Account for Renovation Funds to the Servicer in a timely manner for posting to
elements.label: text

elements.text: the Borrower’s Mortgage account
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Providing to the Servicer for inclusion in the Mortgage file all documentation maintained
elements.label: text

elements.text: by any Outsourced Vendor related to the completion of renovations as required by this
elements.label: text

elements.text: Chapter 4607
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Enforcing the property insurance requirements in Section 4607.13
elements.label: text

elements.text: (b) Prohibited Transfers of Servicing
elements.label: text

elements.text: The following Transfers of Servicing involving CHOICERenovation Mortgages are
elements.label: text

elements.text: prohibited:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Subsequent Transfers of Servicing involving CHOICERenovation In Progress Mortgages
elements.label: text

elements.text: are prohibited until all renovations have been completed, the Seller has obtained a
elements.label: text

elements.text: completion report pursuant to the appraisal requirements in Section 4607.8, Freddie Mac
elements.label: text

elements.text: has acknowledged completion of the renovations pursuant to Section 4607.9(c) and
elements.label: text

elements.text: Freddie Mac has approved the removal of recourse pursuant to Section 4607.14. (See
elements.label: text

elements.text: Chapter 7101 regarding Transfers of Servicing.)
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Transfers of Servicing involving CHOICEReno eXPress Mortgages are prohibited until
elements.label: text

elements.text: all renovations have been completed and the Seller has obtained a completion report
elements.label: text

elements.text: pursuant to the appraisal requirements in Section 4607.8
elements.label: text

elements.text: CHOICERenovation In Progress Mortgages and CHOICEReno eXPress Mortgages may not
elements.label: text

elements.text: be sold through Cash-Released XChange®. Additionally, CHOICEReno eXPress Mortgages
elements.label: text

elements.text: may not be involved in any other Concurrent Transfers of Servicing.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Chapter 4701: Mortgage Insurance
elements.label: section_header

elements.text: 4701.1: Mortgage insurance (02/04/26)
elements.label: section_header

elements.text: This section contains requirements related to:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Mortgage insurance coverage options
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Custom mortgage insurance options
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Mortgaged Premises located in the State of New York
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ MI master policy and approved insurers
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Credit Fees
elements.label: text

elements.text: Mortgage insurance is required for each conventional Mortgage Freddie Mac purchases that has
elements.label: text

elements.text: a loan-to-value (LTV) ratio of more than 80%. The LTV ratio is obtained by dividing the
elements.label: text

elements.text: original loan amount by the value, as defined in Section 4203.1(a).
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Custom mortgage insurance
