elements.text: (b) Custom mortgage insurance options
elements.label: text

elements.text: The custom mortgage insurance option provides an alternative to standard mortgage
elements.label: text

elements.text: insurance coverage.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Custom mortgage insurance is available only for Accept Mortgages.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Custom mortgage insurance may not be financed as part of the principal amount of the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Mortgage.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (c) Mortgaged Premises located in the State of New York
elements.label: text

elements.text: For Mortgaged Premises located in the State of New York, the “value” is the appraised value
elements.label: text

elements.text: of the Mortgaged Premises on the Note Date of the Mortgage and is used solely for the
elements.label: text

elements.text: purpose of determining whether mortgage insurance is required or should be canceled. This
elements.label: text

elements.text: definition of the “value” of Mortgaged Premises located in the State of New York applies
elements.label: text

elements.text: only to the above-stated mortgage insurance requirements and is not applicable for any other
elements.label: text

elements.text: purposes under the terms of the Purchase Documents. After determining that mortgage
elements.label: text

elements.text: insurance is required, the definition of “value” from Section 4203.1(a) should be used for the
elements.label: text

elements.text: purposes of selecting the appropriate mortgage insurance coverage level.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Note: For special requirements related to Cooperative Share Loans when the Cooperative
elements.label: text

elements.text: Unit is located in the state of New York, see Section 4701.5.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (d) MI master policy and approved insurers
elements.label: text

elements.text: The Mortgage must be insured by an approved private MI or State housing finance agency
elements.label: text

elements.text: that is approved to insure Mortgages as of the Delivery Date of the Mortgage (see Exhibit 10,
elements.label: text

elements.text: Freddie Mac-Approved Mortgage Insurers). The Mortgage must be insured under an
elements.label: text

elements.text: approved master policy or loan loss reserve agreement in accordance with Exhibit 10.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Mortgage insurance coverage must continue to be carried with the MI that insured the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Mortgage, except as provided for transfers of mortgage insurance coverage in Section
elements.label: text

elements.text: The required mortgage insurance must be in full force and effect as of the Delivery Date and
elements.label: text

elements.text: remain in force until canceled in accordance with the requirements of Sections 8203.2
elements.label: text

elements.text: through 8203.4(c) or pursuant to applicable law. Mortgage insurance coverage must not be
elements.label: text

elements.text: subject to any exclusion besides those exclusions stated in the relevant master policy or loan
elements.label: text

elements.text: loss reserve agreement. Coverage must run to the benefit of Freddie Mac for a whole loan or
elements.label: text

elements.text: a participation loan insured under a participation policy or to the Seller for any other insured
elements.label: text

elements.text: participation loan. No action may have been taken, or no action may have failed to be taken,
elements.label: text

elements.text: that would impair the rights of Freddie Mac or the Seller. Participation policies with
elements.label: text

elements.text: provisions inconsistent with this section or that impose premium payment or reporting
elements.label: text

elements.text: requirements on Freddie Mac are not acceptable.
elements.label: text

elements.text: The Seller warrants that the Borrower has been given all disclosures required by law,
elements.label: text

elements.text: including, but not limited to, the Homeowners Protection Act of 1998 (HPA), as amended,
elements.label: text

elements.text: relating to the terms on which Borrower-paid mortgage insurance may be canceled. This

elements.text: 4701.2: Mortgage insurance premiums (02/04/26)
elements.label: section_header

elements.text: This section contains requirements related to:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Eligible mortgage insurance premiums
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Borrower-paid financed premiums
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Lender-paid mortgage insurance
elements.label: text

elements.text: (a) Eligible mortgage insurance premiums
elements.label: text

elements.text: Eligible Mortgage insurance premiums include the following:
elements.label: text

elements.text: Eligible mortgage insurance premiums
elements.label: text

elements.text: Premium type When premiums are paid
elements.label: text

elements.text: Annual premium An initial premium paid at closing to cover the first year’s
elements.label: text

elements.text: premium and annual renewal premium payments thereafter paid
elements.label: text

elements.text: from accumulated escrow deposits
elements.label: text

elements.text: Monthly premium Premiums paid monthly from accumulated escrow deposits
elements.label: text

elements.text: Single-premium A lump-sum premium paid at closing to purchase life of
elements.label: text

elements.text: Mortgage coverage
elements.label: text

elements.text: Split-premium An initial up-front payment premium paid at closing and an
elements.label: text

elements.text: ongoing monthly premium paid from accumulated escrow
elements.label: text

elements.text: deposits
elements.label: text

elements.text: (i) Borrower-paid mortgage insurance premiums
elements.label: text

elements.text: For Borrower-paid mortgage insurance premiums, the Borrower must pay the mortgage
elements.label: text

elements.text: insurance premium by a single payment at closing, through monthly Escrow payments or
elements.label: text

elements.text: as a combination of the two. A Mortgage that includes a Borrower-paid mortgage
elements.label: text

elements.text: insurance premium in the Note Rate is not eligible for sale to Freddie Mac.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (ii) Lender-paid mortgage insurance premiums
elements.label: text

elements.text: Lender-paid mortgage insurance premiums for annual and monthly premium programs
elements.label: text

elements.text: must be included in the Servicing Spread included in the Note Rate on the Mortgage (see
elements.label: text

elements.text: Section 4701.2(b)). Mortgages with single-premium lender-paid mortgage insurance do
elements.label: text

elements.text: not require an adjustment to the Minimum Servicing Spread.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (b) Borrower-paid financed premiums
elements.label: text

elements.text: For purposes of this section, the definitions below apply.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Definitions applicable to this section
elements.label: text

elements.text: B
elements.label: text

elements.text: Base LTV ratio The loan-to-value (LTV) ratio calculated using the Mortgage
elements.label: text

elements.text: amount without the financed mortgage insurance premium
elements.label: text

elements.text: G
elements.label: text

elements.text: Gross LTV ratio The LTV ratio calculated using the Mortgage amount, which
elements.label: text

elements.text: includes the financed mortgage insurance premium
elements.label: text

elements.text: Mortgages for which the Borrower-paid mortgage insurance premium is included as part of
elements.label: text

elements.text: the principal amount of the Mortgage (i.e., a financed premium) are eligible for purchase
elements.label: text

elements.text: provided the Mortgage complies with the following requirements:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ The Base LTV ratio must not exceed the maximum LTV ratio permitted for the Mortgage
elements.label: text

elements.text: Product or offering
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ The Gross LTV ratio must not exceed 95%, except for Home Possible® Mortgages,
elements.label: text

elements.text: HomeOne® Mortgages and HeritageOne® Mortgages, for which the Gross LTV ratio
elements.label: text

elements.text: must not exceed 97%
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ The Mortgaged Premises must be a 1- to 4-unit Primary Residence or a 1-unit second
elements.label: text

elements.text: home
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ The Mortgage is a fixed-rate, fully amortizing Mortgage or an ARM
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ The amount of coverage meets the standard coverage level requirements in
elements.label: text

elements.text: Section 4701.1 using the Base LTV ratio
elements.label: text

elements.text: (i) Financed mortgage insurance premium endorsement
elements.label: text

elements.text: The mortgage insurance policy must include an endorsement, generally referred to as the
elements.label: text

elements.text: “financed mortgage insurance premium endorsement.” This endorsement states that
elements.label: text

elements.text: adjustments will be made to the claim calculation to meet the required exposure level for
elements.label: text

elements.text: the Base LTV ratio.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (ii) Maximum original loan amount
elements.label: text

elements.text: The maximum original loan amounts provided in Section 4203.1(c) apply to Mortgages
elements.label: text

elements.text: with financed mortgage insurance premiums. The original loan amount of the Mortgage
elements.label: text

elements.text: inclusive of the amount of any financed mortgage insurance premium may not exceed the
elements.label: text

elements.text: maximum original loan limits provided in Section 4203.1(c).
elements.label: text

elements.text: (iii)Delivery requirements for Mortgages with financed mortgage insurance premium
elements.label: text

elements.text: See Section 6302.21 for delivery requirements. Any applicable Credit Fees will be
elements.label: text

elements.text: assessed based on the Mortgage’s Gross LTV ratio and the UPB, which includes the
elements.label: text

elements.text: financed mortgage insurance premium.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (c) Lender-paid mortgage insurance
elements.label: text

elements.text: Freddie Mac will purchase Mortgages with single, annual or monthly premium lender-paid
elements.label: text

elements.text: mortgage insurance that meet the requirements below.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (i) Annual and monthly premiums
elements.label: text

elements.text: For annual and monthly premiums:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ The Mortgage must be a fixed-rate, fully amortizing Mortgage or a non-convertible
elements.label: text

elements.text: ARM
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ For monthly and annual premium programs, premium payments must be made from
elements.label: text

elements.text: the Servicing Spread compensation.
elements.label: text

elements.text: To ensure that the Servicer receives sufficient Servicing compensation after premium
elements.label: text

elements.text: payments are made:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ❑ For fixed-rate Mortgages, the Minimum Contract Servicing Spread must be at
elements.label: text

elements.text: least 0.250% and the maximum Servicing Spread may not exceed 0.500%. The
elements.label: text

elements.text: Minimum Contract Servicing Spread must be equal to or greater than the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Minimum Servicing Spread. The Minimum Contract Servicing Spread must be no
elements.label: text

elements.text: less than the sum of the Minimum Servicing Spread plus the amount necessary to
elements.label: text

elements.text: pay the mortgage insurance premium when due and must not exceed 0.500%.
elements.label: text

elements.text: ❑ For non-convertible ARMs, the Minimum Contract Servicing Spread
elements.label: text

elements.text: requirements in Sections 6102.4(b) and 6201.4(b)(iii) must be met
elements.label: text

elements.text: ❑ For Mortgages that are modified in accordance with Chapter 9206, the Servicing
elements.label: text

elements.text: Spread will be reduced to 0.250% in accordance with Section 9206.2(d)(iii). This
elements.label: text

elements.text: potential change in the Servicing Spread does not change a Servicer’s
elements.label: text

elements.text: responsibility to continue making premium payments.
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Coverage must be maintained for the life of the Mortgage. A change in MI may be
elements.label: text

elements.text: allowed if approved by Freddie Mac (see Chapter 8203).
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ The Mortgage must be sold under the Guarantor or MultiLender Swap program
elements.label: text

elements.text: (ii) Single premiums
elements.label: text

elements.text: For single premiums:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ The Mortgage must be a Mortgage eligible for purchase under the Purchase
elements.label: text

elements.text: Documents
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Coverage must be maintained for the life of the Mortgage. A change in MI may be
elements.label: text

elements.text: allowed if approved by Freddie Mac (see Chapter 8203).
elements.label: text

elements.text: The Seller must ensure that the required mortgage insurance for the Mortgage is in full force
elements.label: text

elements.text: and effective on the Delivery Date of the Mortgage regardless of whether the entire mortgage
elements.label: text

elements.text: insurance premium is paid by the Seller prior to the Delivery Date. The Seller must obtain
elements.label: text

elements.text: and be able to produce evidence of any required mortgage insurance (including, but not
elements.label: text

elements.text: limited to, a certificate of insurance).
elements.label: text

elements.text: Note: See Section 6302.21 for delivery requirements for Mortgages with lender-paid
elements.label: text

elements.text: mortgage insurance.
elements.label: text

elements.text: 4701.3: Commissions, fees or other compensation on
elements.label: section_header

elements.text: insurance (03/02/16)
elements.label: text

elements.text: The Seller warrants that in connection with the placement or renewal of any mortgage insurance,
elements.label: text

elements.text: including insurance on any other Mortgages it owns, to the Seller’s knowledge, the insurer
elements.label: text

elements.text: (including its parent company or any affiliate thereof) has not caused or permitted any
elements.label: text

elements.text: consideration or thing of value (other than the protection provided by its mortgage insurance) to
elements.label: text

elements.text: be paid to or received by any of the following:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ The Mortgage lender

elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Any insurance agency, corporation (other than the insurer), partnership, trust or other

elements.text: business entity (including any service corporation, whether organized for profit or otherwise)

elements.text: in which the lender or any of its officers, directors, employees or their immediate family

elements.label: text

elements.text: insurance presently in force or to be placed in the future.

elements.text: The Uniform Single Family Note provides blanks for inserting the amounts of late charges and
elements.label: text

elements.text: the grace period after which such charges are assessable. Any amount and period stated must be
elements.label: text

elements.text: permissible under applicable law.
elements.label: text

elements.text: For Mortgages purchased by Freddie Mac, the Seller agrees to collect late charges only on
elements.label: text

elements.text: monthly installments more than 15 days late. If the 15-day period ends on a weekend or holiday,
elements.label: text

elements.text: it is extended to the next Business Day. The Seller also agrees not to collect late charges of more
elements.label: text

elements.text: than 5% of the late principal and interest payment. The Seller may retain any late charge
elements.label: text

elements.text: collected as additional Servicing compensation.
elements.label: text

elements.text: If the late charge stated in the Note is more than 5% of the principal and interest payment and/or
elements.label: text

elements.text: is to be assessed on a monthly installment late 15 days or less, the Seller agrees to notify the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Borrower in writing of Freddie Mac’s late charge and grace period requirements and to retain a
elements.label: text

elements.text: copy of the written notification in the Mortgage file for each Mortgage purchased by
elements.label: text

elements.text: Freddie Mac.

elements.text: Chapter 4702: Title Insurance
elements.label: section_header

elements.text: 4702.1: General Freddie Mac title insurance
elements.label: section_header

elements.text: requirements (04/02/25)
elements.label: text

elements.text: A title insurance policy is mandatory for each Mortgage delivered to Freddie Mac unless
elements.label: text

elements.text: specifically excepted by Section 4702.3 or 4702.4.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Each Mortgage purchased by Freddie Mac must be covered by one of the following:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ A paid-up Mortgage title insurance policy meeting the requirements in Section 4702.2
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ An attorney opinion of title letter meeting the requirements in Section 4702.3, or
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ A certificate of title issued by Iowa Title Guaranty
elements.label: text

elements.text: 4702.2: Title insurance policy requirements (04/02/25)
elements.label: section_header

elements.text: This section contains information related to:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Title insurer
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Amount of protection
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Insured Mortgages
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Title insurance policy forms
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Survey requirements
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Exceptions to survey matters
elements.label: text

elements.text: Each title insurance policy must meet the following minimum requirements:
elements.label: text

elements.text: (a) Title insurer
elements.label: text

elements.text: The title insurance policy must be written by a title insurer legally able to do business in the
elements.label: text

elements.text: jurisdiction where the Mortgaged Premises is located.
elements.label: text

elements.text: The policy must be fully enforceable and protective of the mortgagee’s rights and comply
elements.label: text

elements.text: with all other requirements of this section.
elements.label: text

elements.text: If a preliminary binder or commitment is issued, it must be issued by the same title insurer
elements.label: text

elements.text: that issues the final title insurance policy.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Selection or acceptance of the title insurance company by the Seller must be based solely on
elements.label: text

elements.text: considerations, such as:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ The comprehensiveness of the policy
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ The financial ability of the company to stand behind its commitment
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ The company’s record on settling claims and other considerations normally employed by
elements.label: text

elements.text: institutional investors originating or purchasing Mortgages in the jurisdiction where the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Mortgaged Premises is located
elements.label: text

elements.text: The selection or acceptance must not be based on receipt of any fee or other consideration by
elements.label: text

elements.text: the Seller or its employees, officers or directors.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (b) Amount of protection
elements.label: text

elements.text: The title insurance policy must protect the mortgagee up to at least the current principal
elements.label: text

elements.text: balance of the Mortgage.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (c) Insured Mortgage
elements.label: text

elements.text: The title insurance protection must run to Freddie Mac for Mortgages purchased in their
elements.label: text

elements.text: entirety by Freddie Mac.
elements.label: text

elements.text: If a Mortgage is registered with MERS® and is originated naming MERS as original
elements.label: text

elements.text: mortgagee of record, solely as nominee for the Lender named in the Security Instrument and
elements.label: text

elements.text: the Note, and Lender’s successors and assigns, then the “insured Mortgage” covered by the
elements.label: text

elements.text: title insurance policy must be identified in the title insurance policy as the Security
elements.label: text

elements.text: Instrument given to MERS, solely as nominee for Lender and Lender’s successors and
elements.label: text

elements.text: assigns. However, under no circumstances may MERS be named as an insured in a title
elements.label: text

elements.text: insurance policy.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Furthermore, if a Mortgage is registered with MERS, the Seller/Servicer must arrange for all
elements.label: text

elements.text: insurance drafts, notices, policies, invoices, etc., to be delivered directly to the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Seller/Servicer. Although the MERS address appears in local public land records, the address
elements.label: text

elements.text: for MERS must not be given to organizations that normally direct mail to the Seller/Servicer
elements.label: text

elements.text: or Servicing Agent.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (d) Title insurance policy forms
elements.label: text

elements.text: The title insurance policy must be written on:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Effective for Mortgages with Note Dates up to December 31, 2023, Freddie Mac will
elements.label: text

elements.text: accept either the 2006 (adopted 6/17/06) or 2021 (adopted 7/1/2021) versions of the
elements.label: text

elements.text: following policy forms:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ❑ The American Land Title Association (ALTA) Loan Policy
elements.label: text

elements.text: ❑ The ALTA Short Form Residential Loan Policy One-to-Four-Family or
elements.label: text

elements.text: ❑ The ALTA Expanded Coverage Residential Loan Policy One- to Four-Family
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Effective for Mortgages with Note Dates on or after January 1, 2024, only the 2021
elements.label: text

elements.text: versions of the above policy forms will be accepted
elements.label: text

elements.text: Whichever policy is used, it must be permitted to be issued in the State where the Mortgaged
elements.label: text

elements.text: Premises is located under applicable law (e.g., Seller cannot deliver a 2021 Loan Policy in
elements.label: text

elements.text: New York until the State of New York has approved use of that policy form).
elements.label: text

elements.text: Sellers may use a title insurance policy written on a form other than one of the ALTA
elements.label: text

elements.text: insurance policy forms described above, provided the Seller warrants that the coverage the
elements.label: text

elements.text: policy provides is at least as broad as the coverage provided by the applicable ALTA title
elements.label: text

elements.text: insurance policy.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Regardless of the title insurance policy form used, endorsements must be attached to or,
elements.label: text

elements.text: where applicable, incorporated by reference into the policy.
elements.label: text

elements.text: The following endorsements must correspond to the year of the title insurance policy form
elements.label: text

elements.text: used (e.g., an endorsement used only with the ALTA 2006 Loan policy cannot be used with
elements.label: text

elements.text: the ALTA 2021 Loan Policy):
elements.label: text

elements.text: Endorsements that must correspond to the year of the title insurance policy form
elements.label: text

elements.text: used
elements.label: text

elements.text: An ALTA Form 8.1, Environmental Protection Lien Endorsement. Form 8.1 may make an
elements.label: text

elements.text: exception only for specific State statutes that provide for possible subsequent “superliens”
elements.label: text

elements.text: that could take priority over the Mortgage.
elements.label: text

elements.text: For all ARMs, the appropriate ALTA form for Variable Rate Mortgages (ALTA Form 6,
elements.label: text

elements.text: Variable Rate Mortgage; ALTA Form 6.1, Variable Rate Mortgage — regulations; or
elements.label: text

elements.text: ALTA Form 6.2, Variable Rate Mortgage — Negative Amortization)
elements.label: text

elements.text: An ALTA Form 4 endorsement or its equivalent for each Condominium Unit Mortgage
elements.label: text

elements.text: An ALTA Form 5 endorsement or its equivalent for each Mortgage secured by a Planned
elements.label: text

elements.text: Unit Development (PUD) unit
elements.label: text

elements.text: An ALTA Form 13.1 endorsement or its equivalent for all leasehold Mortgages and
elements.label: text

elements.text: Endorsements that must correspond to the year of the title insurance policy form
elements.label: text

elements.text: used
elements.label: text

elements.text: Community Land Trust Mortgages
elements.label: text

elements.text: An ALTA Form 7.1 endorsement in States where available, or Form 7 endorsement in
elements.label: text

elements.text: other States, or its equivalent for each Mortgage secured by a Manufactured Home. The
elements.label: text

elements.text: title policy must identify the Manufactured Home located on the land and insure against
elements.label: text

elements.text: any loss if the Manufactured Home is not real property.
elements.label: text

elements.text: An ALTA Form 9 endorsement or its equivalent meeting the requirements of Section
elements.label: text

elements.text: 4702.4(d) (3rd bullet) for each Mortgage secured by property subject to a restrictive
elements.label: text

elements.text: agreement or restrictive covenant
elements.label: text

elements.text: A Seller may accept an ALTA Form 47.1 (Operative Law) endorsement for jurisdictions
elements.label: text

elements.text: where State and Native American Indian tribal authorities overlap, and tribal law could
elements.label: text

elements.text: potentially govern real estate interests in the Mortgaged Premises. However, the endorsement
elements.label: text

elements.text: may not contain any exclusions to coverage, which are noted as optional in the endorsement.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Sellers must review the endorsement to ensure that the exclusions to coverage are removed or
elements.label: text

elements.text: not present.
elements.label: text

elements.text: A Seller may accept evidence of title insurance under a master title insurance policy for any
elements.label: text

elements.text: Home Mortgage. Any Seller that accepts evidence of title insurance under a master title
elements.label: text

elements.text: insurance policy represents and warrants as follows:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ The Seller has reviewed the title insurer’s master policy documents, including the
elements.label: text

elements.text: certificate of title insurance or short-form title policy, the master policy with all
elements.label: text

elements.text: endorsements and any other applicable documents, and, based on this review and on
elements.label: text

elements.text: certifications from the title insurer, the Seller has confirmed that the master policy
elements.label: text

elements.text: provides at least the amount and scope of coverage given by the applicable ALTA title
elements.label: text

elements.text: insurance policy and that the master policy otherwise meets the requirements of this
elements.label: text

elements.text: Section 4702.2
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ The Seller has obtained from the title insurer a fully executed master title insurance
elements.label: text

elements.text: policy issued in the Seller’s name as insured and assigns to Freddie Mac its rights in the
elements.label: text

elements.text: policy
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ The master policy has been approved by the applicable State or local authority where
elements.label: text

elements.text: such approval is required
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ The insurer will replace the title insurance certificate with a full individual ALTA or
elements.label: text

elements.text: similar policy upon 10 days’ notice by Freddie Mac
elements.label: text

elements.text: Freddie Mac may refuse to accept the master title insurance policy of any title insurer.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (e) Survey requirements
elements.label: text

elements.text: If the title company insuring the Mortgage or the attorney rendering the opinion of title
elements.label: text

elements.text: requires a survey to remove exceptions to survey matters, the Seller must provide a survey of
elements.label: text

elements.text: the Mortgaged Premises. The survey provided must conform to:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ The title insurance company’s or attorney’s standards, and
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Any applicable legal standards relating to surveys
elements.label: text

elements.text: (f) Exceptions to survey matters
elements.label: text

elements.text: When the title insurance policy takes exception to survey matters, other than those permitted
elements.label: text

elements.text: under Section 4702.4, the Seller must provide whatever information is required by the title
elements.label: text

elements.text: insurance company to either remove the exception or obtain an endorsement providing the
elements.label: text

elements.text: insurance required. If the title company will not issue a policy without a survey exception,
elements.label: text

elements.text: Freddie Mac will not purchase the Mortgage. In addition, the title policy must not be subject
elements.label: text

elements.text: to any title exceptions other than those permitted under Section 4702.4.
elements.label: text

elements.text: 4702.3: Attorney opinion of title letter (02/04/26)
elements.label: section_header

elements.text: This section contains information related to:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Issuer requirements
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Attorney opinion of title letter requirements
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Mortgages that are ineligible for delivery with an attorney opinion of title letter
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Documentation and delivery requirements for an attorney opinion of title letter
elements.label: text

elements.text: (a) Issuer requirements
elements.label: text

elements.text: Attorney must meet the following requirements to be acceptable issuer of attorney opinion of
elements.label: text

elements.text: title letters:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Be licensed and in good standing to practice law in the jurisdiction where the Mortgaged
elements.label: text

elements.text: Premises is located
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Provide coverage for the “gap” period between closing of the Mortgage and recordation
elements.label: text

elements.text: of the loan documents
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Maintain professional liability insurance that meets the following requirements:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ❑ Extends coverage against malpractice or errors and omissions in rendering opinions
elements.label: text

elements.text: of title
elements.label: text

elements.text: ❑ Maintains coverage amount that adequately accounts for the quantity and size of
elements.label: text

elements.text: opinions rendered by the attorney and organization or law firm, without limits of
elements.label: text

elements.text: liability for a single claim lower than the UPB of the Mortgaged Premises
elements.label: text

elements.text: ❑ Extends coverage to all assignees and successors
elements.label: text

elements.text: ❑ Extends coverage to the organization or law firm under which the attorney opinion of
elements.label: text

elements.text: title letter is issued, in the event the attorney is no longer a member of the firm or
elements.label: text

elements.text: organization
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Provide current proof of acceptable professional liability coverage
elements.label: text

elements.text: (b) Attorney opinion of title letter requirements
elements.label: text

elements.text: Freddie Mac will accept an attorney opinion of title letter in lieu of a title insurance policy if
elements.label: text

elements.text: all the following conditions are satisfied.
elements.label: text

elements.text: The attorney opinion of title letter must:
elements.label: text

elements.text: Attorney opinion of title letter requirements
elements.label: text

elements.text: Be addressed to the Seller and all successors in interest of the Seller
elements.label: text

elements.text: Provide the following statement:
elements.label: text

elements.text: “We [I] agree to indemnify you and your successors in interest in the [Mortgage] [deed of
elements.label: text

elements.text: trust] opined hereto, to the full extent of any loss attributable to a breach of our [my] duty
elements.label: text

elements.text: to exercise reasonable care and skill in the examination of the title and the giving of this
elements.label: text

elements.text: opinion.”
elements.label: text

elements.text: Be issued by an eligible attorney and organization as defined above

elements.label: text

elements.text: Planned Unit Development (PUD):

elements.text: ■ There is no violation of any restrictive covenants that are in the Condominium Project

elements.text: or PUD constituent documents and restrict the use of the land

elements.text: ■ All dues applicable to the Mortgages Premises are current and not delinquent
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ No recorded right of first refusal to purchase the land was exercised or could have
elements.label: text

elements.text: Attorney opinion of title letter requirements
elements.label: text

elements.text: been exercised on or before the closing date of the Mortgage and the undersigned is
elements.label: text

elements.text: unaware of the existence or the exercise of any right of first refusal on or before the
elements.label: text

elements.text: closing date of the Mortgage
elements.label: text

elements.text: For ARMs, if applicable, include the following:
elements.label: text

elements.text: “The law of [the State in which the property securing the Mortgage is located] provides
elements.label: text

elements.text: that (i) the lien of the Mortgage will not become invalid or unenforceable resulting from
elements.label: text

elements.text: provisions in the Mortgage which provide for changes in the interest rate calculated
elements.label: text

elements.text: pursuant to the formula provided in the Mortgage, and (ii) priority of the lien of the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Mortgage for the UPB of the loan, together with interest as changed and other sums
elements.label: text

elements.text: advanced by the Noteholder in accordance with the provisions of the Mortgage, will not
elements.label: text

elements.text: be lost as a result of changes in the rate of interest calculated pursuant to the formula
elements.label: text

elements.text: provided in the Mortgage.”
elements.label: text

elements.text: Include an opinion on environmental protection liens that addresses the following:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ That there are no environmental protection liens filed in the public records that have
elements.label: text

elements.text: priority over the lien of the insured Mortgage
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ That there are no applicable State laws that provide that environmental liens filed after

elements.text: the date of the policy would have priority over the lien of the insured Mortgage. If
elements.label: text

elements.text: there are such laws, the letter must expressly identify them as an exception.
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ May include an exception for possible subsequent superliens that could take priority
elements.label: text

elements.text: over the Mortgage only if the Mortgaged Premises is located in a State whose State
elements.label: text

elements.text: statutes provide for such a superlien
elements.label: text

elements.text: Not take exception to survey matters; when the attorney’s opinion takes exception to
elements.label: text

elements.text: survey matters, the Seller must provide whatever information is required by the attorney

elements.text: to remove the exception
elements.label: text

elements.text: Not be subject to any title exceptions other than those permitted under Section 4702.4

elements.text: (c) Mortgages that are ineligible for delivery with an attorney opinion of title letter
elements.label: text

elements.text: An attorney opinion of title letter may not be delivered for Mortgages with the following

elements.text: characteristics:

elements.text: ■ Mortgaged Premises is located in a Tribal Area (as defined in Section 4504.2)

elements.text: prohibited by law

elements.text: ■ Mortgages secured by a dwelling on a leasehold estate, including leasehold estates on

elements.text: ■ Mortgages secured by Manufactured Homes

elements.text: (d) Documentation and delivery requirements for an attorney opinion of title letter

elements.text: 4702.4: Acceptable exceptions to the title insurance
elements.label: section_header

elements.text: policy or to the attorney’s opinion of title letter
elements.label: text

elements.text: This section contains requirements related to:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Subsurface public utility easements
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Surface public utility easements
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Encroachments on public utility easements
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Restrictive agreements and restrictive covenants
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Mutual easement agreements
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Fence misplacements
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Encroachments on the Mortgaged Premises by improvements on adjoining property
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Encroachments on adjoining property
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Oil, gas, water and mineral rights
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Liens for taxes not due
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Sums readvanced
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Tenants in possession
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Liens and leases for solar panels
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Other exceptions
elements.label: text

elements.text: The following exceptions to the title insurance policy or to the attorney opinion of title letter are
elements.label: text

elements.text: acceptable:
elements.label: text

elements.text: (a) Subsurface public utility easements
elements.label: text

elements.text: Exceptions for subsurface public utility easements for local residential distribution, such as
elements.label: text

elements.text: lines for gas and water, and cable for electric, telephone or television utilities, are acceptable
elements.label: text

elements.text: provided that the location of the easements is ascertainable and fixed. The exercise of the
elements.label: text

elements.text: rights thereunder must not interfere with the use and enjoyment of any present improvements
elements.label: text

elements.text: on the Mortgaged Premises or proposed improvements on which the appraisal or Mortgage is
elements.label: text

elements.text: based.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (b) Surface public utility easements
elements.label: text

elements.text: Exceptions for surface easements for public utilities for local residential distribution are
elements.label: text

elements.text: acceptable provided that the location of the easements is ascertainable and fixed. The
elements.label: text

elements.text: exercise of the rights thereunder must not interfere with the use and enjoyment of any of the
elements.label: text

elements.text: following:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Present improvements on the Mortgaged Premises
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Proposed improvements upon which the appraisal or Mortgage is based
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Part of the Mortgaged Premises outside the easement and not occupied by improvements
elements.label: text

elements.text: (c) Encroachments on public utility easements
elements.label: text

elements.text: Exceptions for encroachments on easements for public utilities by a garage, tool shed or
elements.label: text

elements.text: similar structure that is not attached to, or a portion of, the dwelling structure are acceptable
elements.label: text

elements.text: provided that the encroachments do not interfere with the use and enjoyment of the
elements.label: text

elements.text: easements or the exercise of rights of repair and maintenance in connection therewith.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (d) Restrictive agreements and restrictive covenants
elements.label: text

elements.text: Exceptions for restrictive agreements or restrictive covenants of record related to cost, use,
elements.label: text

elements.text: setback, resale restrictions, right of first refusal, minimum size and building materials, and
elements.label: text

elements.text: architectural, aesthetic or similar matters (other than single-family-use restrictions on 2- to 4-
elements.label: text

elements.text: unit properties) are acceptable provided that the following conditions are met:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ The restrictive agreements or restrictive covenants do not create or provide for any lien
elements.label: text

elements.text: that would be prior to the lien of the Home Mortgage nor provide for the elimination of
elements.label: text

elements.text: the lien of the Home Mortgage
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ The terms and provisions of the restrictive agreements or restrictive covenants are
elements.label: text

elements.text: commonly acceptable to private institutional Mortgage investors in the area where the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Mortgaged Premises is located
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ An endorsement to the title insurance policy affirmatively insures that no violation of
elements.label: text

elements.text: any such restrictive agreement or restrictive covenant exists and that any future violation
elements.label: text

elements.text: shall not result in forfeiture or reversion of title
elements.label: text

elements.text: (e) Mutual easement agreements
elements.label: text

elements.text: Exceptions for mutual easement agreements of record that establish a joint driveway or a
elements.label: text

elements.text: party wall are acceptable if such improvements are constructed in any of the following ways:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Partly on the Mortgaged Premises and partly on adjoining property
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Wholly on the Mortgaged Premises, or
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Wholly on the adjoining property
elements.label: text

elements.text: The easement agreement must allow all present and future owners and their heirs, successors
elements.label: text

elements.text: and assigns forever, unlimited use and enjoyment of the driveway or party wall without any
elements.label: text

elements.text: restriction other than restriction by reason of the mutual easement owners’ rights in common
elements.label: text

elements.text: and duties for joint maintenance.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (f) Fence misplacements
elements.label: text

elements.text: Exceptions for fence misplacements on either side of the property line of the Mortgaged
elements.label: text

elements.text: Premises are acceptable provided that neither the misplacement, nor a future correction
elements.label: text

elements.text: thereof, will interfere with the use and enjoyment of any improvements on the Mortgaged
elements.label: text

elements.text: Premises nor with the use and enjoyment of the balance of the Mortgaged Premises not
elements.label: text

elements.text: occupied by improvements. The definition of fence in this section shall not include retaining
elements.label: text

elements.text: walls or other permanent structures.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (g) Encroachments on the Mortgaged Premises by improvements on adjoining property
elements.label: text

elements.text: Exceptions for encroachments on the Mortgaged Premises by improvements on adjoining
elements.label: text

elements.text: property are acceptable provided that the following conditions are met:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ The encroachment must not touch any improvements on the Mortgaged Premises
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ The encroachment must not interfere with the use and enjoyment of any improvements
elements.label: text

elements.text: on the Mortgaged Premises nor with the use and enjoyment of the Mortgaged Premises
elements.label: text

elements.text: not occupied by improvements
elements.label: text

elements.text: (h) Encroachments on adjoining property
elements.label: text

elements.text: Exceptions for encroachments on adjoining property by eaves or other projections attached to
elements.label: text

elements.text: improvements on the Mortgaged Premises, or by structures such as tool sheds, or by a
elements.label: text

elements.text: driveway appurtenant to the Mortgaged Premises, are acceptable provided that there is an
elements.label: text

elements.text: endorsement to the title insurance policy whereby the policy affirmatively insures against
elements.label: text

elements.text: loss suffered by reason of the entry of a decree or court order requiring the removal of the
elements.label: text

elements.text: encroachment.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (i) Oil, gas, water and mineral rights
elements.label: text

elements.text: Exceptions for outstanding oil, gas, water or mineral rights are acceptable if commonly
elements.label: text

elements.text: granted by private institutional Mortgage investors in the area where the Mortgaged Premises
elements.label: text

elements.text: is located, and:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ The exercise of such rights will not result in damage to the Mortgaged Premises or
elements.label: text

elements.text: impairment of the use or marketability of the Mortgaged Premises for residential
elements.label: text

elements.text: purposes and there is no right of surface or subsurface entry within 200 feet of the
elements.label: text

elements.text: residential structure, or
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ There is a comprehensive endorsement to the title insurance policy that affirmatively
elements.label: text

elements.text: insures the lender against damage or loss due to the exercise of such rights
elements.label: text

elements.text: (j) Liens for taxes not due
elements.label: text

elements.text: Exceptions for liens for real estate or ad valorem taxes and assessments that specifically state
elements.label: text

elements.text: that such liens are not yet due and payable are acceptable.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (k) Sums readvanced
elements.label: text

elements.text: This includes the priority of the lien for any sum repaid and subsequently readvanced under
elements.label: text

elements.text: the terms of the Mortgage insured thereby.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (l) Tenants in possession
elements.label: text

elements.text: Exceptions for rights of tenants in possession, as tenants only, under prior unrecorded leases
elements.label: text

elements.text: are acceptable.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (m)Liens and leases for solar panels
elements.label: text

elements.text: Exceptions for liens and leases, including UCC-1 Financing Statements, for solar panels are
elements.label: text

elements.text: acceptable provided the exception is for a lien or lease that lists only the solar panels as the
elements.label: text

elements.text: collateral.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (n) Other exceptions
elements.label: text

elements.text: Any exception not set forth above in Sections 4702.4(a) through 4702.4(m) is acceptable
elements.label: text

elements.text: only if all of the following conditions are met:
elements.label: text

elements.text: 1. The subject of the exception must not interfere with the use and enjoyment of any present
elements.label: text

elements.text: or proposed improvements on the Mortgaged Premises or with the use and enjoyment of
elements.label: text

elements.text: the balance of the Mortgaged Premises not occupied by improvements
elements.label: text

elements.text: 2. The subject of the exception must not affect the marketability of the Mortgaged Premises
elements.label: text

elements.text: 3. The subject of the exception must have no or minimal effect on the value of the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Mortgaged Premises
elements.label: text

elements.text: 4. The subject of the exception must be acceptable to the MI if the Mortgage is insured
elements.label: text

elements.text: 5. The subject of the exception must be commonly acceptable to private institutional
elements.label: text

elements.text: Mortgage investors in the area where the Mortgaged Premises is located
elements.label: text

elements.text: The Seller shall warrant that all exceptions to the title insurance policy or to the attorney
elements.label: text

elements.text: opinion of title letter are permissible under this section. Freddie Mac will not issue any letters
elements.label: text

elements.text: addressing the acceptability of particular exceptions nor waivers of the above requirements.
elements.label: text

elements.text: 4702.5: Special title insurance requirements for Texas
elements.label: section_header

elements.text: Equity Section 50(a)(6) Mortgages (04/02/25)
elements.label: text

elements.text: Texas Equity Section 50(a)(6) Mortgages must be covered by a title insurance policy meeting the
elements.label: text

elements.text: requirements of Chapter 4702 with the endorsements described below:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ An Equity Loan Mortgage Endorsement (Form T-42). The Form T-42 endorsement must
elements.label: text

elements.text: include the optional coverage provided by paragraph 2(f) of the endorsement, and there must
elements.label: text

elements.text: not be any exceptions to, or deletions of, paragraphs 2(a) through 2(e) of the Form T-42
elements.label: text

elements.text: endorsement.
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ A Supplemental Coverage Equity Loan Mortgage Endorsement (Form T 42.1). There must
elements.label: text

elements.text: not be any exceptions to, or deletions of, paragraphs 1(a) through 1(k) of the Form T-42.1
elements.label: text

elements.text: endorsement or any subsequent subparagraphs added to Paragraph 1 by any revision of the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Form T 42.1 approved by the Texas Insurance Commission.
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Any other endorsement that provides additional optional mortgagee coverage that is
elements.label: text

elements.text: approved by the State of Texas Insurance Commission. The endorsement must be obtained
elements.label: text

elements.text: by the Seller for Texas Equity Section 50(a)(6) Mortgages originated on and after the date
elements.label: text

elements.text: the endorsement becomes legally available. There must be no exceptions to, or deletions of,
elements.label: text

elements.text: any paragraphs providing additional coverage in any such endorsement.
elements.label: text

elements.text: 4702.6: Title insurance requirements for Cooperative
elements.label: section_header

elements.text: Projects and Cooperative Share Loans (04/02/25)
elements.label: text

elements.text: This section contains requirements related to:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Cooperative Projects
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Cooperative Share Loans
elements.label: text

elements.text: (a) Cooperative Projects
elements.label: text

elements.text: The Cooperative Project must be covered by a title insurance policy that meets the
elements.label: text

elements.text: requirements of the Seller’s Purchase Documents.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (b) Cooperative Share Loans
elements.label: text

elements.text: When a Cooperative Share Loan is recognized as real property, a title insurance policy is
elements.label: text

elements.text: required to evidence that:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ The Shareholder has good and marketable title to the Shares, and
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ The Cooperative Corporation has good and marketable title to the Cooperative Project
elements.label: text

elements.text: When a Cooperative Share Loan is recognized as personal property, the Seller represents and
elements.label: text

elements.text: warrants that:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ The Shareholder has good and marketable title to the Cooperative Shares
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ The Cooperative Corporation has good and marketable title to the Cooperative Project,
elements.label: text

elements.text: and
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ A title lien search was obtained prior to closing or delivery of the Cooperative Share
elements.label: text

elements.text: Loan and a copy is retained in the Mortgage file
elements.label: text

elements.text: 4702.7: Title insurance requirements for HeritageOne®

elements.text: The following title insurance requirements apply to HeritageOne® Mortgages:

elements.text: HeritageOne Mortgages

elements.text: Land ownership interest Title insurance requirements

elements.text: Tribal trust land or allotted

elements.text: Chapter 4703: Property Insurance
elements.label: section_header

elements.text: 4703.1: General property insurance requirements
elements.label: section_header

elements.text: For Mortgages sold to Freddie Mac, the Seller must ensure that the Mortgaged Premises are
elements.label: text

elements.text: covered by insurance meeting the requirements in this Chapter 4703. The Seller must obtain
elements.label: text

elements.text: evidence of insurance meeting the requirements of Section 8202.4.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (a) Licensing of insurer
elements.label: text

elements.text: All insurance companies (insurers) and insurance companies which guarantee coverages
elements.label: text

elements.text: provided by other insurance companies (reinsurers) must be licensed, or otherwise authorized
elements.label: text

elements.text: by law, to conduct business in the jurisdictions where the Mortgaged Premises are located.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (b) Assessments
elements.label: text

elements.text: Insurance contracts must provide that no assessment may be made against the Seller/Servicer
elements.label: text

elements.text: or Freddie Mac and that any assessment made against others may not become a lien on the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Mortgaged Premises superior to the lien of the Freddie Mac Mortgage.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (c) Rating of insurer
elements.label: text

elements.text: The required insurance must be provided by one of the following insurers:
elements.label: text

elements.text: 1. An insurer with a current rating that meets the requirements below:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ For an insurer rated by AM Best Company (AM Best), a minimum Financial Strength
elements.label: text

elements.text: Rating of B+, as reported online at http://www.ambest.com
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ For an insurer rated by Demotech, Inc., a minimum Financial Stability Rating of A as
elements.label: text

elements.text: reported online at http://www.demotech.com
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ For an insurer rated by S&P Global, a minimum Insurer Financial Strength Rating of
elements.label: text

elements.text: BBB as reported online at http://www.standardandpoors.com
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ For an insurer rated by Kroll Bond Rating Agency (KBRA), a minimum Insurance
elements.label: text

elements.text: Financial Strength Rating of BBB as reported online at https://www.kbra.com
elements.label: text

elements.text: 2. An insurer with coverage that is guaranteed by a reinsurer under all of the following
elements.label: text

elements.text: conditions:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ The reinsurer’s current rating meets the requirements below:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ❑ For a reinsurer rated by AM Best, a minimum Financial Strength Rating of B+; or
elements.label: text

elements.text: ❑ For a reinsurer rated by S&P Global, a minimum Insurer Financial Strength
elements.label: text

elements.text: Rating of BBB
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ The reinsurer assumes by endorsement 100% of the insurer’s liability for any covered
elements.label: text

elements.text: loss payable but unpaid by the insurer for reason of insolvency
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ The reinsurer assumes by endorsement to give the policyholder, the Seller/Servicer
elements.label: text

elements.text: and insurer 90-day written notice before canceling or otherwise terminating the
elements.label: text

elements.text: guarantee
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ The above endorsements are attached to each property insurance policy accepted by
elements.label: text

elements.text: the Seller/Servicer on account of the endorsements
elements.label: text

elements.text: 3. A state insurance pool created by statutory authority to provide insurance for geographic
elements.label: text

elements.text: areas or insurance lines which suffer from lack of voluntary market availability (such
elements.label: text

elements.text: pool may be designated as a property insurance plan, a Fair Access to Insurance
elements.label: text

elements.text: Requirements (FAIR) plan, an underwriting association, a joint underwriting association
elements.label: text

elements.text: or an insurance authority)
elements.label: text

elements.text: 4. A non-admitted insurer with a current rating that meets the requirements below:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ For an insurer rated by AM Best, a minimum Financial Strength Rating of A, or
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ For an insurer rated by S&P Global, a minimum Insurer Financial Strength Rating of
elements.label: text

elements.text: AA￾5. An insurer with coverage that is guaranteed by the National Flood Insurance Program
elements.label: text

elements.text: (NFIP) under a Standard Flood Insurance Policy issued pursuant to the National Flood
elements.label: text

elements.text: Insurance Act of 1968, as amended
elements.label: text

elements.text: Insurers rated by more than one rating company need only meet one of the rating
elements.label: text

elements.text: requirements.
elements.label: text

elements.text: 4703.2: Minimum property insurance types and
elements.label: section_header

elements.text: amounts (05/06/26)
elements.label: text

elements.text: (a) 1- to 4-unit properties
elements.label: text

elements.text: (i) Peril requirements
elements.label: text

elements.text: At a minimum, the insurable improvements on the Mortgaged Premises must be insured
elements.label: text

elements.text: for loss or damage from:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Fire
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Lightning
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Windstorm (including named storms designated by the U.S. National Weather
elements.label: text

elements.text: Service or the National Oceanic and Atmospheric Administration by a name or
elements.label: text

elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Building ordinance or law endorsement – not required if the building is legally
elements.label: text

elements.text: conforming under current building, zoning or land use laws or is not available;
elements.label: text

elements.text: however, it is required if the enforcement of any law or ordinance results in increased
elements.label: text

elements.text: costs such as demolition or loss to the undamaged portions of the building and the
elements.label: text

elements.text: coverage is available in the insurance market
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Steam boiler and machinery or equipment breakdown endorsement – required if a
elements.label: text

elements.text: building in the Cooperative Project has a central heating ventilation and cooling
elements.label: text

elements.text: system and the coverage is available in the insurance market.
elements.label: text

elements.text: The insurance limit per covered mechanical breakdown or equipment failure must
elements.label: text

elements.text: equal the lesser of:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ❑ 100% of the replacement cost of the building housing the equipment, or
elements.label: text

elements.text: ❑ $2 million
elements.label: text

elements.text: (v) Deductible requirements
elements.label: text

elements.text: The master property insurance policy deductible(s) for all perils required by Section
elements.label: text

elements.text: 4703.2 may not exceed 5% of the limit maintained for building(s) coverage per
elements.label: text

elements.text: occurrence.
elements.label: text

elements.text: If the master property insurance policy includes a per unit deductible, the deductible may
elements.label: text

elements.text: not exceed $50,000 per unit.
elements.label: text

elements.text: The cooperative corporation may purchase a deductible buy-back insurance policy to
elements.label: text

elements.text: meet Freddie Mac’s deductible requirements, provided the policy meets all other
elements.label: text

elements.text: applicable property insurance requirements in Chapter 4703.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (e) HO-6 policy requirements
elements.label: text

elements.text: (i) Determining when a unit owner property insurance policy in a project is required
elements.label: text

elements.text: The Seller/Servicer must document that the Borrower has an HO-6 unit owners policy
elements.label: text

elements.text: when the master property insurance policy:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Does not cover all or any portion of the interior of the unit or the improvements to the
elements.label: text

elements.text: unit, or
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Includes a per unit deductible
elements.label: text

elements.text: (ii) Coverage sufficiency requirements
elements.label: text

elements.text: The HO-6 coverage limit amount must be at least equal to the greater of:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ The amount sufficient to repair the unit to at least its condition prior to the loss, or
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ The amount of the per unit deductible, if the master property insurance policy
elements.label: text

elements.text: includes a per unit deductible
elements.label: text

elements.text: (iii) Peril requirements
elements.label: text

elements.text: At a minimum, the insurable improvements on the Mortgaged Premises must be insured
elements.label: text

elements.text: for loss or damage from:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Fire
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Lightning
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Windstorm (including named storms designated by the U.S. National Weather
elements.label: text

elements.text: Service or the National Oceanic and Atmospheric Administration by a name or
elements.label: text

elements.text: number)
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Hail
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Explosion
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Riot
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Civil commotion
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Aircraft
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Vehicles
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Smoke
elements.label: text

elements.text: Additionally, if a master property insurance policy includes a per unit deductible, then the
elements.label: text

elements.text: HO-6 policy must include coverage for all applicable perils to which the per unit
elements.label: text

elements.text: deductible applies.
elements.label: text

elements.text: If any of the preceding perils is excluded from the primary insurance policy, coverage of
elements.label: text

elements.text: the excluded peril must be provided through a secondary insurance policy.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (iv) Loss settlement requirements
elements.label: text

elements.text: HO-6 policies must provide for coverage on a replacement cost basis.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (v) Deductible requirements
elements.label: text

elements.text: The HO-6 policy deductible(s) cannot exceed the greater of 5% of the coverage limit or
elements.label: text

elements.text: (vi) Mortgagee clause requirements
elements.label: text

elements.text: In the event the HO-6 unit owner policy is required, the policy must include the standard
elements.label: text

elements.text: Mortgage clause required in Section 4703.6.
elements.label: text

elements.text: 4703.3: Flood insurance (06/10/26)
elements.label: section_header

elements.text: (a) Determining if a property requires flood insurance
elements.label: text

elements.text: A flood zone determination (FZD) must be made for each property securing a Mortgage sold
elements.label: text

elements.text: to Freddie Mac.
elements.label: text

elements.text: An FZD must be documented by a completed FEMA Standard Flood Hazard Determination
elements.label: text

elements.text: Form, FEMA Form 086-0-032 (Exhibit 13, Standard Flood Hazard Determination Form
elements.label: text

elements.text: [SFHDF]) in accordance with federal law. The SFHDF may be used in a printed,
elements.label: text

elements.text: computerized or electronic manner and must be retained for the life of the Mortgage in either
elements.label: text

elements.text: hard copy or electronic format. Any alternative electronic format must contain all mandatory
elements.label: text

elements.text: fields indicated on the SFHDF.
elements.label: text

elements.text: The date in the “Date of Determination” field on the SFHDF must be a date that is no more
elements.label: text

elements.text: than 120 days before the Note Date of the Mortgage, or, if applicable, the Note Date of the
elements.label: text

elements.text: refinance Mortgage. However, the “Date of Determination” field on the SFHDF may be a
elements.label: text

elements.text: date that is more than 120 days before the Note Date of the Mortgage or, if applicable, the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Note Date of the refinance Mortgage, if the Seller uses a “life of loan” FZD where the third
elements.label: text

elements.text: party providing the FZD provides a “life of loan” certificate assuring that the Mortgage is
elements.label: text

elements.text: monitored for compliance.
elements.label: text

elements.text: The loan number or other identifying information in the “Loan Identifier” field on the
elements.label: text

elements.text: SFHDF must be the loan number or other identifying information for the Mortgage or, if
elements.label: text

elements.text: applicable, the refinance Mortgage.
elements.label: text

elements.text: The Seller/Servicer warrants that any FZD made by a party other than the Seller/Servicer is
elements.label: text

elements.text: guaranteed by the FZD maker to be accurate, in accordance with federal law. The
elements.label: text

elements.text: Seller/Servicer, however, remains responsible to Freddie Mac for the accuracy of any FZD
elements.label: text

elements.text: made by the Seller/Servicer or any party other than the Seller/Servicer.
elements.label: text

elements.text: If the SFHDF identifies the insurable improvements on the Mortgaged Premises as located in
elements.label: text

elements.text: an area that has been identified as a Special Flood Hazard Area (SFHA) containing the letter
elements.label: text

elements.text: “A” or “V” within its designated zone on a flood map (Flood Hazard Boundary Map or Flood
elements.label: text

elements.text: Insurance Rate Map) of FEMA, the Seller/Servicer must ensure that flood insurance is
elements.label: text

elements.text: obtained and maintained on such improvements for the term of the Mortgage.
elements.label: text

elements.text: The Seller/Servicer may waive or discontinue the flood insurance requirement if:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ The Borrower and the Seller/Servicer have obtained, following a joint request to FEMA
elements.label: text

elements.text: as provided under federal law, a Letter of Determination Review (LODR) concluding that
elements.label: text

elements.text: the insurable improvements are not in the SFHA, or
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ The Borrower has provided the Seller/Servicer with a Letter of Map Amendment
elements.label: text

elements.text: (LOMA) from FEMA excluding the insurable improvements or the entire property from
elements.label: text

elements.text: the SFHA, or
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ The Borrower has provided the Seller/Servicer with a Letter of Map Revision (LOMR)
elements.label: text

elements.text: from FEMA removing the community’s SFHA designation
elements.label: text

elements.text: The Borrower must maintain flood insurance on the insurable improvements until FEMA
elements.label: text

elements.text: issues a LOMA, LOMR or LODR. Upon issuance of a LOMA, LOMR or LODR, the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Borrower may request from FEMA a refund of paid flood insurance premiums through the
elements.label: text

elements.text: insurance agent servicing the flood insurance policy. A copy of the LOMA, LOMR or
elements.label: text

elements.text: LODR, as applicable, must be maintained in the Mortgage file in accordance with Chapters
elements.label: text

elements.text: If the insurable improvements on the Mortgaged Premises are located in an SFHA but the
elements.label: text

elements.text: community does not participate in the National Flood Insurance Program (NFIP)
elements.label: text

elements.text: (“nonparticipating community”), the Mortgage is not eligible for sale to Freddie Mac.
elements.label: text

elements.text: If the insurable improvements on the Mortgaged Premises are located in an area that has not
elements.label: text

elements.text: been mapped by FEMA and the Seller/Servicer is not aware of any flood risks to which the
elements.label: text

elements.text: improvements are exposed, the Mortgage is eligible for sale to Freddie Mac without the
elements.label: text

elements.text: benefit of flood insurance. If the area has not been mapped by FEMA but the Seller/Servicer
elements.label: text

elements.text: is aware that the insurable improvements are exposed to flood risks, the Mortgage is not
elements.label: text

elements.text: eligible for sale to Freddie Mac without flood insurance on the improvements.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (b) Acceptable flood insurance policies
elements.label: text

elements.text: The flood insurance policy may be one of the following:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ A standard policy issued by the NFIP, or
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ A policy issued by a private insurer that is qualified under Section 4703.1, with at least
elements.label: text

elements.text: equivalent terms and conditions to the standard NFIP policy for the types of
elements.label: text

elements.text: improvements insured, including coverage, deductibles and exclusions and conditions
elements.label: text

elements.text: offered
elements.label: text

elements.text: (c) Coverage required
elements.label: text

elements.text: (i) 1- to 4-unit properties
elements.label: text

elements.text: If the community where the Mortgaged Premises are located participates in the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Emergency Program of the NFIP, the flood insurance coverage on the insurable
elements.label: text

elements.text: improvements must at least equal the lowest of the following:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ The UPB of the Mortgage
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ The maximum amount of coverage currently sold under the Emergency Program of
elements.label: text

elements.text: the NFIP for the type of improvements insured
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ The replacement cost of the insurable improvements
elements.label: text

elements.text: The Seller/Servicer must ensure that the Borrower increases flood insurance coverage on
elements.label: text

elements.text: the insurable improvements when the community moves into the Regular Program of the
elements.label: text

elements.text: NFIP as described below.
elements.label: text

elements.text: If the community where the Mortgaged Premises are located participates in the Regular
elements.label: text

elements.text: Program of the NFIP, the flood insurance coverage on the insurable improvements must
elements.label: text

elements.text: at least equal the lowest of the following:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ The UPB of the Mortgage
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ The maximum amount of coverage currently sold under the Regular Program of the
elements.label: text

elements.text: NFIP for the type of improvements insured
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ The replacement cost of the insurable improvements
elements.label: text

elements.text: The deductible may not exceed the maximum deductible amount currently allowed under
elements.label: text

elements.text: the NFIP for the type of improvements insured.
elements.label: text

elements.text: For 1- to 4-unit properties, the Seller/Servicer may waive the flood insurance
elements.label: text

elements.text: requirements for structures on the Mortgaged Premises that are detached from the
elements.label: text

elements.text: primary residential structure and do not serve as a residence.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (ii) PUD or ground lease community units
elements.label: text

elements.text: Flood insurance requirements for 1- to 4-unit properties apply to similar residential
elements.label: text

elements.text: properties within a PUD or ground lease community.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (iii) Condominium Units
elements.label: text

elements.text: Flood insurance requirements for 1- to 4-unit properties apply to similar residential
elements.label: text

elements.text: properties in a 2- to 4-Unit Condominium Project or Detached Condominium Project.
elements.label: text

elements.text: If the Condominium Unit securing a Mortgage sold to or serviced for Freddie Mac is in a
elements.label: text

elements.text: building in a Condominium Project other than a 2- to 4-Unit Condominium Project or
elements.label: text

elements.text: Detached Condominium Project and all or part of the building is in an SFHA, the
elements.label: text

elements.text: following flood insurance coverage, as applicable, is required:
elements.label: text

elements.text: A. Condominium homeowners association’s coverage
elements.label: text

elements.text: The condominium homeowners association must maintain building coverage on the
elements.label: text

elements.text: building for the lower of:
elements.label: text

elements.text: (i) 80% of the building’s replacement cost or
elements.label: text
